name: Kirk Cameron
subject: Bring wholesome messaging back to children’sentertainment
body:
Please – for the sake of all our families

| | | You are absolutely right if you think Hollywood is reaching right into your family living room with "entertainment" that grooms children.
---
| |
---
| | Dear Friend in Faith,Please – for the sake of all our families – take it from me as someone who’s lived and worked in Hollywood my whole life... You are absolutely right if you think Hollywood is reaching right into your family living room with “entertainment” that grooms children.You may remember shows with wholesome messages like Father Knows Best, Marcus Welby, M.D., and even Dennis the Menace.But today when children turn on the TV, they see a Drag Queen leading the Pride Parade on _Blue’s Clues_...
---
[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zfoogo1ous913ir9or1992ogddnf06ne5g3qi6n0_rp22sh2s8i6eor5cphm2ob4cdh2bs)
---
| | Or Sesame Street using YOUR tax-dollars to normalize same-sex parents and men in dresses.And the result is an upside-down world where children lecture YOU about what you can and cannot say... ...and they try to “educate” YOU about your “old-fashioned” and “mean” ideas.Today you have a very special chance to stop the Hollywood groomers who are turning our children and grandchildren against us and the Godly values we love. **[Today, I’m inviting you to be a Founding Supporter of the very first children’s television show that actively fights back against the groomers in Hollywood AND reinforces the traditional biblical values that made America great.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z0trgm9vnrklsanoikjmnkojfkaq894r99fv89b8_rp22sh2s8i6eor5cphm2ob4cdh2bs)** Introducing: _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_.I’m making this ground-breaking live-action television show with the great folks at **Brave Books**.If you’ve seen me and **Brave Books** on _Fox News_ , you know we’ve already countered those awful “Drag Queen Story Hours” the Left has been pushing on children in our taxpayer funded libraries. Thanks to support from parents, grandparents, and concerned citizens just like you, we started hosting Godly and patriotic “Story Hours” of our own.And now, THOUSANDS of children and parents can gather to pledge allegiance to our flag, read wholesome books, and pray together in their libraries.It’s encouraging to see we can win when we fight back! Now we’re working to have that same impact in television.
---
>> Imagine a children's show that leaves your child or grandchild with a greater sense of right and wrong and an understanding of truth.Courage, honesty, discernment, forgiveness, empathy, all taught through a biblical and traditional lens. >> Imagine a children's show that brings families together instead of driving them apart.>> Now imagine YOU are the one who makes that show possible. Because we can’t beat the Hollywood groomers without your help as a **Founding Supporter**.
---
The great news is that we’ve already completed and released the first ten character-building episodes of _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_! We premiered it recently to a small group of children and parents and I wish you could have seen the smiles on those innocent little faces.And I wish you could have experienced _the parents_ watching their children absorb the beautiful Gospel messages.It was quite a moment for everyone – including folks just like you – who worked so hard to get us that far. But now **Brave Books** has an urgent two-fold problem that I hope you can help with.
---
_**#1. Brave Books needs to launch a massive advertising blitz to get around the groomers on the Left.**_
---
You see, we’ve made the first five episodes of Season 1 of _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ available absolutely FREE. That’s the best way we can reach MILLIONS of families with our Gospel-inspired messaging.But no one will see it if we can’t get the word out. And the Left will try to bury _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ at every turn. The price tag for an advertising blitz of this magnitude is $250,000 – and with the recent launch, we need to raise the funds immediately.
---
_**#2. We must complete Season 2 to keep our momentum going and that cost is $250,000 for post production.**_
---
I know that’s a lot of money but it’s actually a fraction of what it would cost if we made our show with a major Hollywood studio. And obviously, we won’t work through Hollywood.Hollywood has done quite enough already to destroy American culture and hijack our children.We parents, grandparents, and concerned citizens must take over to rebuild a thriving, healthy, GOOD and GODLY culture. **[That’s why I’m asking you to please contribute $35, $50, $250, $500, $2,500 or more to help us reach our fundraising goal of $250,000 for advertising and $250,000 for Season 2.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zq750stpi5756mpietcn8gjlo4un22qjmh4p42lg_rp22sh2s8i6eor5cphm2ob4cdh2bs) ** As our special “Thank You” for becoming a **Founding Supporter** at the $50 level, we’ll send you all ten episodes of Season 1 of _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ FREE on DVD.This exclusive _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ DVD is only offered to **Founding Supporters**. It won’t be available for sale anywhere. So with **[**your gift of $50**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zcgnoabvtk4ojb686dhm26319ciq2d2gkkldro18_rp22sh2s8i6eor5cphm2ob4cdh2bs)** , you’ll be part of a small select group of people who own our wholesome TV show on DVD. You’ll have it ready to watch with the children in your life. Or you can drop your DVD by your local school, church, mission, or even leave it in the waiting room at your doctor’s office. So many children have no way of hearing God’s message in their lives. Your random act of kindness may change a child’s life. **[So please – whether you can send $50, $5,000, $25,000, or anything higher or lower, please respond quickly and know that gifts of EVERY size are needed and greatly appreciated.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z17j6osp9ch684r8q9l2t738utnuv6brn1gfg8e0_rp22sh2s8i6eor5cphm2ob4cdh2bs)** There’s no time to waste. Hollywood groomers have destroyed America’s Godly culture. But today we rebuild from the ashes.And it starts by ensuring the most innocent among us are filled with faith and family – not filth.Thank you for being a part of history by becoming a **Founding Supporter** of _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_.
---
Sincerely,
---
| | Kirk Cameron (and Iggy!)Actor, Author, and Father
---
| [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zqctsi1915ggv7vs5mifjuguvhukpnhmoe1uv588_rp22sh2s8i6eor5cphm2ob4cdh2bs)
---
| | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z2nk0r94mtq0gohfl5df6s42158j5lddhe557n00_rp22sh2s8i6eor5cphm2ob4cdh2bs)
---
**Kirk Cameron (and Iggy!)** **Actor, Author, and Father**
---
|
---
| | P.S. The #1 thing you can do today to stop the Hollywood groomers who are tearing families apart – maybe even yours – is to support the very first children’s television show in _history_ made possible by contributions from grandparents and seniors like you. **[Please send in your Founding Supporter gift and help me reclaim America with the Biblical values our country was founded upon.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zvagbg49agk04q73c7e6libbhor6pens2bgh9518_rp22sh2s8i6eor5cphm2ob4cdh2bs)** Thank you and God bless you and your family! -- Kirk
---

---

name: Troy Downing
subject: This is a dangerous line they should never have crossed
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Troy Downing for Congress
body:
Should these Democrat Senators be tried for TREASON?

[](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)Peter,

Democrat Senators did something that should absolutely ENRAGE every patriot: They released a video openly urging our men and women in uniform to IGNORE orders from their Commander-in-Chief.[](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)Let that sink in.

The same liberal elites who spent years screaming about “insurrection” and “threats to democracy” are now encouraging our troops to undermine the military’s chain of command. This is reckless and irresponsible.
These senators aren’t clueless freshmen; they’re long-time members of the Senate Armed Services and Intelligence Committees.
They know what happens when you sow doubt and disobedience inside our military. And they’re doing it on purpose.
[Peter, should these Democrat Senators be tried for TREASON?](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)[YES — LOCK THEM UP
](https://trk.emails.troydowning._CNdhv7yFBJg)[NO — LET THEM GET AWAY
](https://trk.emails.troydowning._yWfLQ)America cannot afford leaders who undermine our national security. These Senators are counting on your silence, but patriots must make their voices heard NOW.
Stand up, speak out, and show that Americans will never tolerate an attack on our military or our Commander-in-Chief. [Take our 5-second poll today.](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)

Thank you for your patriotic support,

Team Downing| |

subject: Why I’m endorsing Trisha's campaign to beat Lauren Boebert
body:
Chip in $25 or more to help Trisha beat Lauren Boebert, win in CO-04 and flip the House blue.

---
| Hi Peter. I'm Scott Kelly - former NASA Astronaut, retired U.S. Navy Captain and resident of CO district 4.
I've been to space 4 times and spent nearly a year on the International Space Station (ISS). I commanded both the Space Shuttle and the ISS and flew with more than 40 people from across the world--scientists, engineers, doctors--men and women of every background, faith, and nationality. We didn't share the same backgrounds, but we shared a mission: work together, stay focused, and deliver.
That's the kind of leadership and focus we need in Congress. That's what Trisha Calvarese brings to the table.
I'm proud to endorse Trisha Calvarese in Colorado's 4th District.
Trisha is running to defeat Lauren Boebert - and I'm proud to endorse her in this critical mission.
Trisha grew up here on the rural Eastern Plains and in Douglas County. She served our country as a civil servant and returned home to care for her sick parents at the end of their lives. She understands the people of this district because she's actually one of us. She's running for Congress to stand up for the dignity and care of families in her hometown.
That means protecting Social Security and Medicare. It means working to bring down the cost of everything from prescription drugs to groceries. It means investing again in U.S. science and engineering to create good-paying jobs and opportunities. Trisha's ready to actually show up, listen and deliver for folks in her district.
But while Trisha is advocating for you, Lauren Boebert is spending her time making headlines, avoiding her constituents, and voting to gut the programs Coloradans rely on. That's why I'm counting on you to join me in supporting Trisha's campaign to defeat Boebert.
But grassroots campaigns like this one aren't easy. And right now Trisha is counting on our help to raise the $10,000 she needs to build the early infrastructure it takes to win races like this.
[So, I'm personally asking: Will you chip in $25 or more today to help Trisha Calvarese defeat Lauren Boebert, take back the House for Democrats and give working families the voice they deserve in Congress?](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Vh5DukWYH3qGzSa3Ne11Sq7jL5fSZEmC3qPCrjFFNk0LtpzdylsQo0LN_TLhv8DeTiXdwHPposbvjbMGHE3hNfEnU9MqW1BMrX0h-sqF0g1wN8XFvx0gcu9bA2ZQTeuUZMVqGsY1fsP0Vmr76YiU7-TAl0hRVuzBTOIUUPLum57cvAeLK4-kOISwhxXT-XSu542aKclhOmNP9fcweQbDdHZXjLVTFg0BJtFNCq1KukM=&c=snDlTkRGJZPuG5tw74vxnHChaWgPGzsJ0a_8NUQp7qgWwd_U55h1WQ==&ch=dGOwDb46F7iDzbQ6CibhaG09lZmzrvCBsxYsU-nG8vflW2hbR7CJIw==)
---

| Hi Peter. I'm Scott Kelly - former NASA Astronaut, retired U.S. Navy Captain and resident of CO district 4.
I've been to space 4 times and spent nearly a year on the International Space Station (ISS). I commanded both the Space Shuttle and the ISS and flew with more than 40 people from across the world--scientists, engineers, doctors--men and women of every background, faith, and nationality. We didn't share the same backgrounds, but we shared a mission: work together, stay focused, and deliver.
That's the kind of leadership and focus we need in Congress. That's what Trisha Calvarese brings to the table.
I'm proud to endorse Trisha Calvarese in Colorado's 4th District.
Trisha is running to defeat Lauren Boebert - and I'm proud to endorse her in this critical mission.
Trisha grew up here on the rural Eastern Plains and in Douglas County. She served our country as a civil servant and returned home to care for her sick parents at the end of their lives. She understands the people of this district because she's actually one of us. She's running for Congress to stand up for the dignity and care of families in her hometown.
That means protecting Social Security and Medicare. It means working to bring down the cost of everything from prescription drugs to groceries. It means investing again in U.S. science and engineering to create good-paying jobs and opportunities. Trisha's ready to actually show up, listen and deliver for folks in her district.
But while Trisha is advocating for you, Lauren Boebert is spending her time making headlines, avoiding her constituents, and voting to gut the programs Coloradans rely on. That's why I'm counting on you to join me in supporting Trisha's campaign to defeat Boebert.
But grassroots campaigns like this one aren't easy. And right now Trisha is counting on our help to raise the $10,000 she needs to build the early infrastructure it takes to win races like this.

And after Indiana, the floodgates are poised to burst open with Florida next in line to rig their maps. We are watching a coordinated nationwide assault designed to lock in a MAGA-controlled Congress for up to a decade. Think about what that means. A decade of draconian abortion bans. A decade of gutting social safety nets like SNAP and Social Security. A decade of MAGA violence, mass arrests, and worse. Fair Fight Colorado is fighting back to prevent this nightmare from becoming reality. We are leading the charge for a redistricting ballot measure in Colorado to counter MAGA's election rigging scheme. But MAGA is going to fight us with everything they got, and the fascist billionaires bankrolling their takeover have endless pockets. == If you've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: | | == | == | == | | == | == | == | == We cannot let MAGA steal our future. -- Fair Fight Colorado | | | | == | | == | Donate by mail: Fair Fight Colorado == == | Paid for by Fair Fight Colorado. body:
The Trump administration [reached a settlement](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkbGO2zAMhp9G3hhIlBwng4cUhbfrckNHQ7aoiwpZNCQlht--UNE3uJH4CYLf9__-mD9siCF9_bIbjdXznOgokWqlPJf6cpRqZJv2aJO3b86h0vqcUWIPCkHJ2dkQz9l0bvRESLqjUQ3XXl_N9aa654huNfber86i1ne9UK8NusHKwdz9groLY7umUEmlpDHyMuhbP9w9eimX27JaYSS5UDlf_nBI9Uk-nvymfFl56-L4rHUvQj8ETgKn4zguW0ktEzhRglcROG2c6BQ4Nbg2hhgDpwLs4T8kNEpYOGc-KBdY-RUdLASe80oOQqoMmXZ7bm27-SjwSo4yJDqgUK2RWiRwshkeD_Wp4uaEnngNTuifP5pkOson5b3Lo9uP9kS5DsLIr82G-A-nfquD94h_AwAA___vQJ78) with Missouri on Tuesday, ending former President Biden's Saving on a Valuable Education (SAVE) repayment plan, forcing 7 million borrowers to choose new repayment options.

| | |

| [](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkbluG0EMQL9mtpMwx14qtghgqHPalAQ9y9EymWMxQ8nw3wcbKwFSu34PjyD54xVekSPn23dMtEgokOm9RRKhCk3uK2WJBfMeMQd8lMpCfgOr7XAy9mQ0rMjxA_puXQKRJdfRYqZxcGM_zqbbFmNo1qPz_Rgufp5pQndBTxjM4HEwvuPlqBlrtDG67_V5cvMwXYINWr_Nbx5Vr2llKfX8s3CWjUL8KA-qZ19SF5dNZG_KfVP2quyVc-PbJu3cElbB1kgOT9lr4IzZM8YTrg9upZ4SN8Ff1JS73iVBK_fqSbmXZx9yVHY8iMe0I9_yJwMIGD34koWyQMB_naf9CZR7meAvgshNQMr-dIRq-m8SHAewQ1eXdX_nGLmNk-r1LSHHP4vKl77zWOzvAAAA__8jV6sK) | |
# Wednesday, December 10, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1906, President Theodore Roosevelt, famous for his bellicose rhetoric, [won the Nobel Peace Prize](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkTsO2zAQRE8jdWuQFPUrVKRR57QphZW5lDagSIPcmHBOH9hXSPswmMHD_Lpvd-TA8fiJFy3i0xaplkAilLcifxxFCQnjM2D0-EqZhR7nZpTpQRvQanPI4b3Z1i2eyFDX0qLHoe8GO0y6PRfvyPvBKpo9dXZy_dRbq3ar53Ew1u4tL582bbTSWlmrbmM39ePsjVdqn_YHNlaRY0n59jtxlJN8eKcX5dsjXW1YTpFnabofjVkbs9Zab-ViOUuKjPHC4xNrzFouzAIfu8asKYKcXMDhGziCntUACDHtFOCZ-S9BxQJYMTtyIAkwAl6U-YERfMogJ4HnXASEL_oiio7jAQgVM3wHCJKclKGwI0j-S46QdgI9qXnq-8E2Zm3z4p6VQ-AyjI1Vx4Ucvm7yX4e8FvMvAAD__yexoXg)--the first American to win any Nobel Prize--for helping end the Russo-Japanese War of 1905. It turns out being a magnetic personality isn't really about you--it's about making the other person feel seen. In today's _Wisdom Wednesday_ , we're looking at [the simple trick](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGu4yAQAL8Gd0SADSYFxTXucu2V1tos8V4wRGaTyH9_uvc-4dUjzUjz5zbfgDKV-2_YMXCqc8FPy8iMx9z4FbFwrlCeGUqCdz2Icd1mo4yV2kit5giUz3noYkiIBvsOgx6d7d3gvO620Ednk_Le9y4lHRdUaPS6XMFbt1iVOgr_bdpopbUaBnUZe2_HazJJqcUvK4hBYSSux-VvpcIbpnzWNx6Xte5dDhvzs4n-lzCTMFM8HrSn2vibCzNZyVROucFC3CRvwHKHB8qzviSVxlA4n3KHe0GmVZipO0J8fihnam4Ug7rvQPkrxj869A7mXwAAAP__D958Hg) behind using someone's name. If you're serious about securing a more confident retirement, understanding how to choose the right financial advisor is one of the smartest steps you can take. Today's sponsor, [SmartAsset](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkbluG0EMQL9mtpMwx14qtghgqHPalAQ9y9EymWMxQ8nw3wcbKwFSu34PjyD54xVekSPn23dMtEgokOm9RRKhCk3uK2WJBfMeMQd8lMpCfgOr7XAy9mQ0rMjxA_puXQKRJdfRYqZxcGM_zqbbFmNo1qPz_Rgufp5pQndBTxjM4HEwvuPlqBlrtDG67_V5cvMwXYINWr_Nbx5Vr2llKfX8s3CWjUL8KA-qZ19SF5dNZG_KfVP2quyVc-PbJu3cElbB1kgOT9lr4IzZM8YTrg9upZ4SN8Ff1JS73iVBK_fqSbmXZx9yVHY8iMe0I9_yJwMIGD34koWyQMB_naf9CZR7meAvgshNQMr-dIRq-m8SHAewQ1eXdX_nGLmNk-r1LSHHP4vKl77zWOzvAAAA__8jV6sK), can help you do just that.
|

## Trump Ends Biden Student Loan Plan
The Trump administration [reached a settlement](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkbGO2zAMhp9G3hhIlBwng4cUhbfrckNHQ7aoiwpZNCQlht--UNE3uJH4CYLf9__-mD9siCF9_bIbjdXznOgokWqlPJf6cpRqZJv2aJO3b86h0vqcUWIPCkHJ2dkQz9l0bvRESLqjUQ3XXl_N9aa654huNfber86i1ne9UK8NusHKwdz9groLY7umUEmlpDHyMuhbP9w9eimX27JaYSS5UDlf_nBI9Uk-nvymfFl56-L4rHUvQj8ETgKn4zguW0ktEzhRglcROG2c6BQ4Nbg2hhgDpwLs4T8kNEpYOGc-KBdY-RUdLASe80oOQqoMmXZ7bm27-SjwSo4yJDqgUK2RWiRwshkeD_Wp4uaEnngNTuifP5pkOson5b3Lo9uP9kS5DsLIr82G-A-nfquD94h_AwAA___vQJ78) with Missouri on Tuesday, ending former President Biden's Saving on a Valuable Education (SAVE) repayment plan, forcing 7 million borrowers to choose new repayment options. The Education Department agreed not to enroll new borrowers in SAVE, deny pending applications, and move current participants into [other legal plans](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL1uhDAQhJ_GdKD1Yn6ucJGG7tKmRIu9PhwZG4EPxNtHRHmDlKORvtE3X8_xST74-PqkhXV2aYx87oFz5m3c89tyzCFRXANFR0fafGYzjwjYlBJLCaMlH65RFVY7ZuS6YC27tqlb1faymHWDZEwPtXMNSdt29Khdy6xgggmtMoXXN02iBClBKai6um-6h0MHMPWTIaGArc9pq76Tj3lmF6508FaZtBRBzzmvu6g_BA4Ch_M8KxMnc5cChxstcJAocICHwGGng8s_sfI2K1e6ljus9N65mvMSik3b9fQh-L3thILXQj78ruV_XXRo_AkAAP__Z9V6Qw). SAVE offered lower monthly payments--often $0--and faster debt forgiveness timelines than other income-driven plans, with the Congressional Budget Office estimating it would [cost $230 billion](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykzztuhDAQANDTmG6RPZjPFi7S0G3alGhsxstExkYwC9rbR8kVcoEnva_H9EBOnJ-fuJKTWKZM15FIhPbpkNdMWVLBvCXMEc-ys1BYJtDQ3gzcjJ5m5PSebDW7SATUVORM37VNZ7vBVItDD9GjniEEGgC7GKK1ZMkH07RgsGL3qxkw2hhtra77Zmj7e4SotR98QGU1zSxlr78LZ1kopnc5aa9DWavkFpHtUM2HglHBeF1XHXypn-VUMG4vnzigcMkKxna4D021u3m7OCU-ul5Z_VyR0x8l__qfDn4CAAD__4yqbzk) over a decade. Borrowers have been in forbearance since courts blocked the program in 2024 after seven GOP states sued, claiming Biden overstepped his authority. Here's a way to [calculate new student loan repayment plans](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0E1uwyAQQOHT4F0sGH6cLFh041267dLCZoinwhDhiaPcvkrVG_QCT5_e13W6BspUbp9hQ8-pTgWfe0ZmbNPOj4iFcw3lnkNJ4aiNGJd1Agn2pOCk5BQD5ddkuugTIqDu0KvBWe2MO6tu9aBwtnbQix3cRc1aLcZonGNyTs_W2Y78u6ZASaWkMbIf9NkOlwRJyvk8L0EYiZG4tv67UuEVU37VA1u_1K3LfmW-70J_CBgFjH_mQLG_1UPA-NafdtoeOXBtAsau-Xh_Us60u0EYedsC5d8W_2vA4eEnAAD__zPlb08).
## Australia Cuts Teens Off from Social Media
Australia's first-in-the-world [social media ban](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OqzAUhJ8GOiP_gUnh4jbpctst0cE-Dl4d7Mg2QXn7FfsKW400I81ovq_H8oBIMT3_w462hbwkPCtha1iW2g6PqVGG9CJIAd65xIZuWySXIxOSCb54iPRZdO9tQJSoerTCTKOa9DSLfrPKm1V6VHLUsCoEH5SbRFAG55s309xHe7UJKbgQXGs-GDWP5hZk4HydVwed5uhjy2X4zjG1DQN98hvL4PLek91ae9VO_evkvZP38zyHtLrrxZVfTi7kO3mHGuGSo7YCFIERHMltWNknH21jNbsIxHb0EdgKiRWXQOrJKN4X619nJIp1Mp3mzx0i_c63PzF7W_kTAAD__-XBgeo) for users under 16 is taking effect today, requiring platforms to deactivate more than 1 million accounts. Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, YouTube, and other platforms must take "reasonable steps" to prevent underage users from creating or maintaining accounts, facing fines of up to $33 million for violations. See [which platforms are banned](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGOpDAQRU9jMpBtaGgCB5uQ9aYbosIuQ62M3WMXIG4_6rnCxF__6en9e80voEBx_Qs7GvZpjniVgMyY58KHw8ghQXwHiB7OlInRbrOW-lErXSs5O6Bwz13ljEfU2FZo1NA_2r7rn6razOBbWHqUo3LStnocOq972Y-Ds3JUy1iR-dCUVlIp2XWyGdrnYxi99lIuz8WC6CQ64pSb_4kib-jDnU7MjU17FczG_C6i_SP0JPR0XVeDBTzy3azpbOAQeoIlHVwfReiJojsK57vOuB4BmFIUeirJEoR6R0dQw4p1xsKZ7Gf-vDx8lSob974oBCr9IDq57kDhx4F_Fe40-jsAAP__McSERg). The law, which Australian officials say will be "the world's first domino," aims to protect children from addictive algorithms, cyberbullying, and online predators, with 77% of Australians supporting the measure. See the [Australian teens' perspective here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGOtCAUBeCnwW4M98qgFhR_Yzd_uyVBuCi7DDjKSubtN-4rbHeSk3zJOR8P_TAhhrT8N09SxWedqB6RSqFdH-XbUSoxm7RFk7w58x4K2VUjx_sN8AZcOxPiW4vGKU-E1DWkoJf3Tgo5QLMq9Fza3ktBgsB0xgJ1M6Afh2GUzosmqEsDBA7AheBt3w33fvToOZ-H2RomOLlQ8t5-5pDKSj6-80l7a_OziWotZTtY94_hxHCqtbbzbK-O4XSNYTidwVG-gv1axterjmKB3OzKbTXEGA7ZM8GXpwnx1yx_OuJU-BMAAP__zfxxxQ). While the U.S. has no federal ban, [at least eight states](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLuupSAUhp8GOwws8VZQTGN3pp3SICy3a4JgYLnNefvJPq8w_X_58v35Wr8cRUqv3-5Ey3teEz41IjOWtfIdMHHMLl3Rpd29cyFGf6ygoJcapFZrcBS_V9MEuyMCdg1aPQ59N5hh0s1hZ616Nat5M6bzuldjt3mE2SuDE27gG7KfNQ1aaa2MUe3YTf0477ArtU2bd8IoDMS5tH8zJT5wj9_5jaX1-WyiPZivKrpfAhYBy_M87XlHpsqOsb2rgIVSpYBFwPJ5ErBoJWCZBCxIr4PlT7RKTM6zPCnlImv25KI8MZCTm0tVBqwXMUqf78Jy_xRrU2y4HoqR6jAKo16no_iDxf_l8m3hXwAAAP__zMeJIQ) have passed laws restricting children's access to social media, although many face court challenges on First Amendment grounds.
## Prison Foils Crab Boil Scheme
Guards at [South Carolina's Lee Correctional](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O6yAUhJ8Gd1hw_BcXFLdxl9tuiY7hEJPFYAGJlbdfeV9hu5FGGs33fd31HX3w8fEfd1LVJR3pLIFqpaxLfVmKNSSMR8Do8J2yr2Q2DQIGLoFLoS368NF9Y5UjAuoaUnIah27sx5tsNjU7NyONA1p0wrgB0M0wm1WC6CRJaLy61iRIIaXoe9FO3W2YZgdOiPW2GmS9IOtryu0z-Vg3cuGT3pRbk_YmqK3Wo7DuH4OFwXKeZ2vWclFcPYPligwWm1MkbnM6CjcZVx7oUXiphN98x-yfL4zIS3rVjRvMKfiI_Mi-pMhgabKyx-lD8GWcWC8eO_rwe6D-ydpbwU8AAAD__4_Fg_E) Institution intercepted a drone delivery Sunday containing steak, crab legs, Old Bay seasoning, marijuana, and cigarettes. The Department of Corrections posted about the bust on social media, using the hashtag #ContrabandChristmas, and stated, _"Folks were planning an early holiday Old Bay crab boil and steak dinner."_ The prison spokeswoman quipped that inmates expecting the package are probably "crabby." See the [photo of the contents here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGO2zAQBNCvkbo1SEqWfIWKNO4ubUphKS6tza25Askzob8PnHxC6gFmBu_X5_qJLJweP_FJS426JmpFqFbKa6nfgVIVxXQIpogvzVxp21dn3BWsA2vWgCznOvZhiUSOhp4WO0_XYRqnm-335UY0O0d2Mn5wbiP00U_XEGjzBsl89Ly826yzxlozjuYyD7fr_BFdNMbf_IbdaChw1Xz5rZzqTlFOfVG-bPrsZdlrPUo3_OjcvXP31tqlBYrvsHP3kDURhKxHgS2jB6FHgVIJvwBTgEYUgFNVKPpdd9gwq3BCODIXTRBVvgo0ygRvgsTpAZiAMMsJuwoHPEElgMfz34BXls7d-7yEo7EIl2nuRvN4Isvfx_W_mF-L-xMAAP__JzOWTQ). Flying a drone near a South Carolina prison is a misdemeanor carrying 30 days in jail, while dropping contraband [is a felony](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGu3CAQRb8Gd1gwxjavoEjj7qVJsaU1NoNNwoIFs7vav482v5D6SlfnnNv3-o0xxXz8xDs5DmXN9GqJmKmujR-eMqeC-UqYAz5LjUz7uYKCUWqQWq0eY3qvpvMuEAENHTk9T-Mwmcnq7nT2C0cyEDYYRm2U0iOiBfB2sMZ77bvoPm8atNJaGaP6ebDj_BUgKLXZbUdhFPnIpfa_S8x8Ukjv8qTa7-XeJXcyX00MPwQsAhbc9o9Bf5TPLGC5UaxewPKKlX5xqW8Bi68lk_S1XE02Jvwj94qbTHQ0edXYSqYqA2Fj2cqDT6nBAowA0FXnr1dMKbZpFkYdd4zpHwj_V72ng78BAAD__9t4hCQ) with up to 10 years behind bars. No arrests have been made yet.

Working with a financial advisor can be a crucial part of any healthy retirement plan.

Research suggests that people who work with a financial advisor [feel more at ease about their finances](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkb2O2zAQBp-G6mzwT6KuUBHg4O7SpiT2qKW1CX8Ecq3DvX3g2AmQ-uoZzGLx_Xjzb0CJyvU7ZFw4Vl_woydkxuY731YsnCqUPUGJcNRGjGHzWurxpPRJSb8CpU9vh3WJiBrNgIty02gmO81q2BalnJmdGa2ancQxRIRJWzNrZ-2Lns1Ay72mtJJKSWvl2Zl5dC9RRynf5_cAwkpciWs7_6xUeMOYPuuB7RxqHtKyMe9dmG9CX4S-UOl03bife4bG0Dvy3RP6EqlACQTpBOtBvbZTps7wC7swlxtn3-utBRTm9dn3JQk93UmAvANdy4N5HyEFH2phLOwj_Os87QcQ5tX5v8gn6uy57k-HseX_LnmlpdXj0JZ1_6CUqE9OWHnNQOnPo_yldY5F_w4AAP__vkmpaA) and could end up with about 15% more money to spend in retirement.1 But choosing the wrong one could wreak havoc. I got an email last year from a local Democratic club working to "organize resistance to the Trump administration." On the surface, it sounded fine. Until I read the actual agenda. It was the same tired, identity-based checklist that lost us voters in 2020, 2022, and 2024. A list that claims to represent "the people," yet somehow forgets the actual economic pain that people are living through.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d981b501-db8e-4709-b8d4-6a7e37bda694?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This is the email I received from local Democrats
Not one mention of rising costs. Not one mention of government waste or efficiency. Not one mention of building shared prosperity. Nothing about housing affordability, healthcare costs, childcare costs, or the fact that people in Northeast Ohio cannot even buy a starter home anymore.
It was a list for consultants, not the working class. And it reminded me exactly why I talk about what I call "The Union Method."
* * *
**Unions Fought for Everyone. We Should Too**
When unions were strong in Ohio, they did not divide people by race, gender, pronoun, or label. They fought for workers. Period.
Black, White, Hispanic, Asian. Catholic, Protestant, Jewish. Men and women. Steelworkers in Cleveland. Auto workers in Lordstown.
They fought for higher wages, safer workplaces, better benefits, and time off. They fought to raise the floor for everyone. That was the power of the labor movement. It unified people under a shared economic mission.
Somewhere along the line, Democrats forgot that.
* * *
**The Union Method Means Universal Policy**
Instead of dividing people into a dozen buckets with a dozen targeted programs, the Union Method starts with this simple idea:
When policies lift everyone, the whole country rises.
Universal healthcare.
Lower Crime.
Strong education.
Economic security for working families.
Programs that do not require a flowchart to understand.
Take healthcare. Yes, Black mothers face significantly higher rates of maternal mortality, and Black infants have worse health outcomes. Those are real facts backed by decades of data. But the solution isn't designing a healthcare system around one demographic--it's making sure everyone actually has access to quality care.
States that expanded Medicaid saw maternal and infant mortality drop across all racial groups, and the racial gaps narrowed, even if they didn't disappear entirely. Internationally, countries with universal coverage also show smaller racial and socioeconomic disparities in outcomes.
Better access consistently reduces inequality.
The solution is universal systems, not identity sorting.
* * *
**Housing Should Not Be an Identity Debate**
Everyone in Northeast Ohio needs a roof over their head at a price that does not crush them. That is not an identity issue. That is supply and demand.
Right now the United States is simply not building enough homes. Depending on the model you use, we are short somewhere between about 3.7 million and 4.7 million housing units nationwide. When you have that big of a gap between how many households exist and how many homes are available, prices go up. Since the pandemic, home prices have climbed by roughly 55 percent and rents by more than 30 percent nationwide, with many metros seeing even bigger jumps. That is not because one group is suffering and another is not. It is because the system is undersupplied.
Housing policy that matters is boring and universal. Reform zoning so more duplexes, triplexes, and small apartment buildings can be built in neighborhoods that currently only allow single family homes. Speed up permitting so it does not take years to add new units. Invest in basic infrastructure so more sites are actually buildable. Expand tools that already work, like the Low Income Housing Tax Credit, which has helped finance millions of below market units nationally and requires those units to stay affordable for decades.
In Northeast Ohio, that means building near transit corridors, job centers, and older suburbs that already have roads, schools, and sewers in place. It means making it easier to add an extra unit to a house, convert unused commercial space to apartments, and build mixed income projects instead of fighting every new development because of parking complaints or fear of change.
People do not care what demographic category a housing bill claims to help. They care whether they can pay the rent, keep the mortgage current, and live in a neighborhood that is safe and functional. Identity politics does not fix that. Building more housing and lowering the cost of shelter does.
* * *
**Debt, The Dollar, And Why Families Feel Poorer**
While we are arguing over labels, the math in the background is getting ugly.
As of fall 2025, the national debt is a little over 38 trillion dollars, up more than 2 trillion dollars in a single year. Servicing that debt now costs hundreds of billions of dollars a year in interest payments alone. That is money that cannot go to roads, schools, childcare, or anything else people actually touch in their daily lives.
At the same time, prices have quietly climbed almost 25 percent since before the pandemic. If something cost 100 dollars in 2019, on average it now costs around 125 dollars. Wages are up on paper, but for many families the raise disappears at the grocery store, the gas pump, the pharmacy, and the daycare. Housing costs sit on top of that. The median first time homebuyer is now about 40 years old, the oldest on record, because it takes that long to scrape together a down payment in this environment of high prices and high interest rates.
This is what people feel when they say the dollar is being devalued. It is not about some abstract chart of the exchange rate. It is the fact that the same paycheck buys less food, less housing, less healthcare, and less future than it did five years ago. It is the fact that the government keeps layering on debt and interest while failing to deliver visible improvements in quality of life.

And are we running on that? No.
Are we admitting it is a problem? No. Not Trump. Not Biden. No one is being straight with the people about being slowly cooked like a frog in boiling water with rising costs.
**Union Method politics** can actually change things, instead of just slicing people into groups and giving them slogans.
* * *
**Education is Universal. Opportunity is Universal.**
Universal pre-K.
Better school accountability.
Affordable college and trades.
Economic mobility.
These are not identity programs. These are American programs.
Today, programs like the Earned Income Tax Credit remain some of the most successful anti-poverty tools we have. They are universal. They help everyone who works. They support families. They improve children's futures.
* * *
**Identity Politics Is Not a Strategy. It Is a Distraction.**
For three cycles in a row, Democrats have leaned into identity politics while ignoring the economic collapse happening in front of everyone's eyes. People in Ohio are not furious about pronouns. They are furious about property taxes, healthcare bills, daycare costs, and groceries. They are furious that rent is up, wages are flat, and everything feels harder.
People want the American Dream. A house. Kids. Stability. A future. A country where hard work actually pays off.
Identity-based messaging does nothing to deliver that.
Universal programs do.
* * *
**It Is Time for a Shared American Platform**
We need a political agenda that:
• Delivers universal healthcare or at least a real Medicaid expansion
• Builds housing and real zoning reform
• Funds and strengthens education
• Tackles the US debt and inflation.
• Encourages economic mobility for every worker and every family
This is how you win back working class voters.
This is how you rebuild trust.
This is how you offer a vision that unites instead of divides.
Unions understood that when they fought for workers across every division society tried to impose. They lifted everyone together. They won together. And they built the most prosperous middle class the world had ever seen.
We should try that again.
Let's stop dividing people.
Let's stop running campaigns built on labels.
Let's return to policies that raise the standard of living for everyone.
**This is the Union Method.**
This is the path forward.
This is how Democrats win again.
* * *
Matt Diemer is an **ANGRY DEMOCRAT**. Support him by becoming a paid subscriber and sharing his newsletters.

You win by raising the floor for everyone -- the Union Method. | | | Dec 10 | | | | | | | | | | | | I got an email last year from a local Democratic club working to "organize resistance to the Trump administration." On the surface, it sounded fine. Until I read the actual agenda. It was the same tired, identity-based checklist that lost us voters in 2020, 2022, and 2024. A list that claims to represent "the people," yet somehow forgets the actual economic pain that people are living through. | | ---|---|--- This is the email I received from local Democrats Not one mention of rising costs. Not one mention of government waste or efficiency. Not one mention of building shared prosperity. Nothing about housing affordability, healthcare costs, childcare costs, or the fact that people in Northeast Ohio cannot even buy a starter home anymore. It was a list for consultants, not the working class. And it reminded me exactly why I talk about what I call "The Union Method." Unions Fought for Everyone. We Should Too When unions were strong in Ohio, they did not divide people by race, gender, pronoun, or label. They fought for workers. Period. Black, White, Hispanic, Asian. Catholic, Protestant, Jewish. Men and women. Steelworkers in Cleveland. Auto workers in Lordstown. They fought for higher wages, safer workplaces, better benefits, and time off. They fought to raise the floor for everyone. That was the power of the labor movement. It unified people under a shared economic mission. Somewhere along the line, Democrats forgot that. The Union Method Means Universal Policy Instead of dividing people into a dozen buckets with a dozen targeted programs, the Union Method starts with this simple idea: When policies lift everyone, the whole country rises. Universal healthcare. Lower Crime. Strong education. Economic security for working families. Programs that do not require a flowchart to understand. Take healthcare. Yes, Black mothers face significantly higher rates of maternal mortality, and Black infants have worse health outcomes. Those are real facts backed by decades of data. But the solution isn't designing a healthcare system around one demographic--it's making sure everyone actually has access to quality care. States that expanded Medicaid saw maternal and infant mortality drop across all racial groups, and the racial gaps narrowed, even if they didn't disappear entirely. Internationally, countries with universal coverage also show smaller racial and socioeconomic disparities in outcomes. Better access consistently reduces inequality. The solution is universal systems, not identity sorting. Housing Should Not Be an Identity Debate Everyone in Northeast Ohio needs a roof over their head at a price that does not crush them. That is not an identity issue. That is supply and demand. Right now the United States is simply not building enough homes. Depending on the model you use, we are short somewhere between about 3.7 million and 4.7 million housing units nationwide. When you have that big of a gap between how many households exist and how many homes are available, prices go up. Since the pandemic, home prices have climbed by roughly 55 percent and rents by more than 30 percent nationwide, with many metros seeing even bigger jumps. That is not because one group is suffering and another is not. It is because the system is undersupplied. Housing policy that matters is boring and universal. Reform zoning so more duplexes, triplexes, and small apartment buildings can be built in neighborhoods that currently only allow single family homes. Speed up permitting so it does not take years to add new units. Invest in basic infrastructure so more sites are actually buildable. Expand tools that already work, like the Low Income Housing Tax Credit, which has helped finance millions of below market units nationally and requires those units to stay affordable for decades. In Northeast Ohio, that means building near transit corridors, job centers, and older suburbs that already have roads, schools, and sewers in place. It means making it easier to add an extra unit to a house, convert unused commercial space to apartments, and build mixed income projects instead of fighting every new development because of parking complaints or fear of change. People do not care what demographic category a housing bill claims to help. They care whether they can pay the rent, keep the mortgage current, and live in a neighborhood that is safe and functional. Identity politics does not fix that. Building more housing and lowering the cost of shelter does. Debt, The Dollar, And Why Families Feel Poorer While we are arguing over labels, the math in the background is getting ugly. name: The Texas Flyover
subject: Chip Smuggling Ring Busted, 1,000 Cities Investigated, and Gorillas Return
body:
A Missouri City man was convicted of [orchestrating a scheme](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGu4yAQRb8Gd1jDxLFfCopt3L1tt7QwDAYJQwTj-OXvV85-wrYzV-denT_fy7eJKebtt9lJ848vS6azJWKmutgQn20_tu1K1Ji39WhMTsGCgHepUCpYnInpvQyd0yu4h-pIq2m838Yv-Jq6oKeBpmEEZe7m5m4Trn6yD3JuXD2O3q1d1BdMoQKFoHDolXV2cGa0E9BgAMQA5CKX2nMgph_TfHqXF9Xelr1LOjA_m7j9EjgLnM_z7G1e7fUUOF9sgbNCgTM8BM5Hk4a51EzvJov30ZJs5eBANUsXG9doWRYvP03yWUsje3CpTeZXdNHIjxUZEEAGBSD_GaLrnk0feE9d1e55xpRiGycxwLabmD5r-f8cvzT-DQAA___xQJHj) to smuggle $160 million of export-controlled AI computer chips to China, federal authorities announced Monday. Two others were also arrested in the multi-agency effort, dubbed Operation Gatekeeper.

| | |
---
| [](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0TuO3DAMBuDTyN0YelnyFC4CGNNt2pQCR6JtIvIDEj27e_vAydwgJX98IPj49RE-gDJt809YceCvaQ8bftaMzFhCXOio6znPlyi0zc-zMiYlg5a6uyl9UzIkoPwdbJOGp0x31eCgvOuM62Xvm2WQ2utumqZ7l4yD6FXsFd6nXoExT--eDQ1XM6WVVFoqbVsVU7QJXPQSLUgprMREvJeWF2T8gjrl7_2FpY372uRhYT6qMD-Efgj9oO2FldsCiTbG3AJduXnswoy2s0K7eAgzdlL3QjsQZjSdNVdMSZhRK38V60Wc0O6yk9Du5DXU_SwRhRkLQlxu7ylCZWAMB8TfMOObRlgPoHkTZjyg8IalLnSoXt6s9OHf4c6NGIK1veuc9Mb6pgzp-KScqTovrJxXoPx3Sf6_17wG_ScAAP__fwqcZg) | |
# Wednesday, December 10, 2025

## Supreme Court Declines Book Removal Case
The U.S. Supreme Court on Monday declined to hear a case challenging the authority of the Llano County Public Library to [remove 17 children's books](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGyqyAURb9GO5xzCKIWFK9Jl9fe0kE4KhMUBzAkf3_H_MLt16w9e_08xod23u3Lf72Ryu85jDuV5ClniqNZ3ZG2c1kuIrp9mc6UySKMHHjLkDOE0WrnP6OorZrADliTwk62N9lD39WrIt63NMMshrbX0gprejPNYhDayrm9ydqpS4YcATkgFw0aa4TV0nRAQgNUAsi6HGKTV8r01mn2n_Ci2Jiw1V6tOR-puv2r-L3i91JKc10oRM8LqPg9nUekjZgJZ8xs0vtOlk0hPBPzLmX2dTLvpqjjhyFiJ4a-q6OyR3HeuyS7SsCyaee_k_lvoV6K_wYAAP__tY5-EQ) that contain sexual, obscene, or racial content. The refusal leaves in place an appeals court ruling from May that upheld the removal in 2022. A lower court ordered the library to [return the books](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykUL3SmzAQfBro5JGOXxcq0tA5bUrmkA7Q5ECMdNjm7TPkFb5qi_2Z3f3zGl8YOOzLb9zIyneO406fzCRCaXRrOPJ2LsutSGFfpjMLeaNH0NAoA8ro0WPga6xLbyftn6Yka7q2qdpe9125Wmh6b57koNP93LkGqPKmwko3ru77fi6DvcMMGG1AG6gfxnlXe2xdp6lGrYtakw8S00NWEvpinvmKb0oPF7eS7Spy5KL6VcBQwOCmjGeWsN9sAcM9p4CBo0O-kXGPysVzl0txmBKmS00x_lWJtvhGzgqZ44e8wlkoqTOrfB6JNrpdSZQnx2GnrCSqlTAph5nKZP3xCcwht11R62XDwP_7yc9efVv4FwAA__8pEI8r) to the shelves in 2023, affirming the plaintiffs’ argument that the removal had violated the First Amendment right to free speech. "_If a disappointed patron can't find a book in the library, he can order it online, buy it from a bookstore, or borrow it from a friend_ ," wrote Trump appointee Stuart Kyle Duncan [for the majority](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGSpSAQRb8GM141PTzBgGATstl0Q6sFVGpRLMBx_Pst5xc26uRUn3vvn8_xk2KK-_KbtmDa95zHPVw1hdZCGd0aj7qdy_IQJe7LdNYWvIARAd9cIBcweorpHmXnzQR-EF0wQvXvj16DVt1qaPCzJtBqcKBnKUg6DANp7Z1XM8kumueZQAECQaB8Ceed9NQ7BUESAJMQfGy5vNoaWvimOqc7f4XycnnrkllbOyr7-MXQMrTXdb3OSi17uh-Aoa0tl5uhfZo9h1rMO0P7eBlagQwt6Ac8jxK2wF0-S-M_Kp7iVKjcfMr5L59o545qYGi16vuhf0sAxdB2xfjjiinF2ismYdkopp987f9W_TL4LwAA__8i94nr) in the Fifth Circuit Court ruling. Similar cases [are pending](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL1ugzAQgJ_GbEbnC4EweOjClq4dkX8OONXgyHZC0qevqNS-QNfvO326u4_reDUceJvfzUq6PKc4brTnQKVQGt3Ct7ze5_mYSLzN9p4LeQUjAp6lQqlg9IbDa2wqry34XlWkVdeeT-0FLl21aFCd6T0iTr1S1Ewd9M7ByXrVd-5sLxXrI6ZQgUJQ2NTKedd407oOqDEAogHyXGKqy0KFniZP4RUflGoX1yropZRbFqc3gYPAYd_32tl8XHF4gYOLISbjo8DhoAKHiRN5-ctldkuMQXoym8x3ypICfxlLZflTnEtiV6Q120Ze2hg_j1CVtL_tHALnthMNzKvh8LNW-d8zHxq_AwAA__8Pjott) in other federal appeals courts. If those rulings differ from this one, the Supreme Court could revisit the issue.
## Feds Bust AI Chip Smuggling Operation
A Missouri City man was convicted of [orchestrating a scheme](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGu4yAQRb8Gd1jDxLFfCopt3L1tt7QwDAYJQwTj-OXvV85-wrYzV-denT_fy7eJKebtt9lJ848vS6azJWKmutgQn20_tu1K1Ji39WhMTsGCgHepUCpYnInpvQyd0yu4h-pIq2m838Yv-Jq6oKeBpmEEZe7m5m4Trn6yD3JuXD2O3q1d1BdMoQKFoHDolXV2cGa0E9BgAMQA5CKX2nMgph_TfHqXF9Xelr1LOjA_m7j9EjgLnM_z7G1e7fUUOF9sgbNCgTM8BM5Hk4a51EzvJov30ZJs5eBANUsXG9doWRYvP03yWUsje3CpTeZXdNHIjxUZEEAGBSD_GaLrnk0feE9d1e55xpRiGycxwLabmD5r-f8cvzT-DQAA___xQJHj) to smuggle $160 million of export-controlled AI computer chips to China, federal authorities announced Monday. Two others were also arrested in the multi-agency effort, dubbed Operation Gatekeeper. Alan Hao Hsu [pleaded guilty](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOpDAQRL_GZB61e8EwAcElZHvphajpNthaY4-MZ9j5-xP7CxtVUE8lvfr3OX9SiCFtf2l3Y_1e85zceURXqysz-_A49ue2XUQJaVueR3ViYEbAThvUBmahEN9z28i4gNxN40bT2-7DDjD0jR8B0K4LkBG7iIF-lcVyT0NnDVNL0oTxGjNowCAYbG-GhVshyz24lgBUC05CzeVWvavum441vvPLlRvnvYmjr_VxqI8_CieF03mety-fn1epcKJSA0encLq8rqAactJnLlEUTj4_j5qT5rw_KL316sQVippzegX-QSno6tinHPP21uxDIu1z2vRXTpvCCYdO432gxdJdSz-QbrkDPVhETWBYBHEQhqaM8jhDjOGwvWph2ynEH4f6u-dfI_4PAAD__wVamAE) in October to purchasing more than 6,000 Nvidia GPUs through his Sugar Land company, Hao Global, from a North Carolina vendor. He paid for the purchases using multiple shell companies, falsified shipping documents, and coordinated with two Chinese nationals. "_The stakes are high_ ," [said Nicholas Ganjei](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFuwyAUheGnwRsWENukA0MXb-na0cJwY26CjQXXIXn7Kh36At2Pjn5935fpYjHitnzZFQw9r2naoJYIRJAnF3Av67Es70XGbZmPQuClmJRQPZeKSzF5i_E1dY03s_AfsgEj9dCfhrM46yaY2QvttHJuhpOQg7Y9nK_d7J12g-zt0KB5n0klhVRCqq6VzrvO28FpAZ0VgnUCPFLKLQUgeNpyja_0gNy6tDbRBKK9sNMnUyNTY621vR2F0EG7pAdTY9otU-OemRqPwu1BIWUkhMJLOIj7VDe-2lvK3AXcLI-43cFzi5zABf4HwDegmvK9ycbvFWPEMmjWiWW1GH9b6H-CD6N-AgAA___6KIhe), the U.S. attorney for the Southern District of Texas. "_The country that controls these chips will dominate AI; the country that dominates AI will lead the future_." In July, Chinese nationals [were accused](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkT2O2zAQhU9DdWOQtExpCxVu1ASbIk3SCRQ5kgbLH4McrezbB3KOkPobzMP73u_P6dNSoLT-tBEHfi55SnjUgMxYJrfRo8Z9Xc-LQmmd98rolZy01DdQGpScvKXwmtrGD7P0H6rBQXXmdjW97LtmG1yrzeKW2aprf-27mzFzP3f4sZjOGK1vDQ3nM6WVVFoq3V6U86711rhOYmulFK1ET5zLhTdkfNq6hFf-xnJxOTZh2JgfVVzvQo9Cj8dxXGJNJxN6xAR7FXpkdFvKIa8voUdbmBZyZANQYgyBVkwOhR4j1Zr3QuCIXxBtAi6EHjjDPw8IysgIlMASvPWczG2ULCwzwVzQflXY8l45JwiUvtDDqe6dC_e7-vXnGX80ZfCPg0KgajrRyjVaCu8-_H8rfA_6bwAAAP__00qe2g) of trying to recruit Houston-based members of the U.S. Navy to spy for China and hacking into the University of Texas Medical Branch in Galveston to steal information on COVID-19 vaccines. The following month, a Chinese researcher at MD Anderson tried to share cancer research data with officials in his home country.

Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog—your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | Supreme Court Declines Book Removal Case The U.S. Supreme Court on Monday declined to hear a case challenging the authority of the Llano County Public Library to that contain sexual, obscene, or racial content. The refusal leaves in place an appeals court ruling from May that upheld the removal in 2022. A lower court ordered the library to to the shelves in 2023, affirming the plaintiffs’ argument that the removal had violated the First Amendment right to free speech. "If a disappointed patron can't find a book in the library, he can order it online, buy it from a bookstore, or borrow it from a friend ," wrote Trump appointee Stuart Kyle Duncan in the Fifth Circuit Court ruling. Similar cases in other federal appeals courts. If those rulings differ from this one, the Supreme Court could revisit the issue. Feds Bust AI Chip Smuggling Operation A Missouri City man was convicted of to smuggle $160 million of export-controlled AI computer chips to China, federal authorities announced Monday. Two others were also arrested in the multi-agency effort, dubbed Operation Gatekeeper. Alan Hao Hsu in October to purchasing more than 6,000 Nvidia GPUs through his Sugar Land company, Hao Global, from a North Carolina vendor. He paid for the purchases using multiple shell companies, falsified shipping documents, and coordinated with two Chinese nationals. "The stakes are high ," , the U.S. attorney for the Southern District of Texas. "The country that controls these chips will dominate AI; the country that dominates AI will lead the future." In July, Chinese nationals of trying to recruit Houston-based members of the U.S. Navy to spy for China and hacking into the University of Texas Medical Branch in Galveston to steal information on COVID-19 vaccines. The following month, a Chinese researcher at MD Anderson tried to share cancer research data with officials in his home country. Candidate Fields Set The U.S. Senate race and congressional redistricting have significantly impacted several and local contests. Now that the candidate filing deadline has passed, the field is set for the March primaries. With Ken Paxton for his U.S. Senate seat, State Sens. Mayes Middleton (Galveston) and Joan Huffman (Houston), U.S. Rep. Chip Roy (Austin), and former Department of Justice staffer Aaron Reitz are running to replace Paxton on the Republican ticket. In the Democratic primary, State Sen. Nathan Johnson (Dallas) is running against the former mayor of Galveston and a retired FBI agent. U.S. Rep. | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

name: John McGuire
subject: Terrorists feared me and the left wants me gone
disclaimer_text: Paid for by John McGuire for Virginia
body:
The deep state didn’t break me. Terrorists couldn’t stop me. But I need YOU to hold the line with me now.

If you're a terrorist, you didn't see me coming until it was too late.[](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zc19eiqdfr7rrnv4l26efski4lfqml992305ovig_rp22sh2s8i6eor5cpgeeo71c9h2bs)I was a Navy SEAL sniper.
I've taken shots that don't show up on the news.The missions we ran? Quiet. Surgical. Final.
So no, I don't care what CNN thinks. I care about the country I bled for.
And I care that it's now being handed over to men who've never stood for anything in their lives.
You've probably never heard of me. That's fine. I didn't fight for fame. But you should know this:
My name is John McGuire. I was nearly paralyzed in 2006. They told me I'd never walk again.
I stood. I walked. I ran.
And now I'm running, fighting to stay in Congress, because weak men are trying to finish what our enemies couldn't.
This is a war for the soul of America. And President Trump needs fighters, not consultants.

name: The Editors at Broad + Liberty
subject: Chester County keeps choosing excuses over accountability
body:
News + editorial | Broad + Liberty Weekly Reads | | | |
---
| | | Good morning, and welcome to _Broad + Liberty_ 's Wednesday Weekly Reads!

As always, to stay up to date on late-breaking stories and analysis, [subscribe to our daily email list](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=b2bb389264&e=b7b3eddce2) and get the best of _Broad + Liberty_ delivered straight to your inbox.
---
|
---
|
### **1. ****[Chester County keeps choosing excuses over accountability ](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=d527e042c2&e=b7b3eddce2)**
_By Beth Ann Rosica_ The Chester County Board of Elections’ meeting on Friday to certify the election went down exactly how I expected — lots of emotional public comments, an acknowledgement of the “serious error”, deflected by a statement that voter services staff are “fallible human beings”, culminating in a certification of the election along partisan lines with Democratic Commissioners Josh Maxwell and Marian Moskowitz voting yes and Republican Commissioner Eric Roe voting no. Yet, just because it was what I anticipated does not mean I was happy about it — quite the contrary. **Why It Matters.** Chester County pretends it wants to operate similar to a corporation. It is the only county of its size that employs a Chief Executive Officer, Chief Operating Officer, and Chief Experience Officer (CXO). The CXO was just added this year at an annual cost of over $200,000 to taxpayers — and one of her responsibilities is to supervise voter services! None of these expensive “corporate” positions apparently did anything to prevent such a monumental error. Worse yet, these costly “executives” have not responded in a way consistent with corporate America. A corporation has a board of directors and executives answer to them directly. When a massive mistake is made in a corporation, heads will roll — as they should. [_Continue Reading_](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=81dc8ca389&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
|
---
|
### **2. ****[Delaware County Council encourages economic apartheid ](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=c3747b31a0&e=b7b3eddce2)**
_By Wally Nunn_ Upper Darby and Radnor sit just a few miles apart in Delaware County, Pennsylvania. One is working-class. The other is one of the wealthiest communities in America. Yet the family in Upper Darby pays roughly twice the effective property-tax rate of their neighbor in Radnor — despite earning less than half as much. That single fact exposes the quiet cruelty baked into how Pennsylvania funds schools and local government. **Why It Matters.** For many Upper Darby families earning $50,000 or less, the property tax bill consumes fifteen to twenty percent (or more) of their entire income before utilities, insurance, or groceries. In Radnor, the typical homeowner pays under nine percent of household income in property taxes. And the final insult? Despite shouldering a far heavier burden, Upper Darby School District spent $14,728 per student in 2022. Radnor spent $25,264 — 70 percent more. The system doesn’t just fail to be progressive. It is aggressively regressive. Lower property values trigger higher effective tax rates, which forces working-class towns to tax their residents harder just to deliver basic services. Wealthier towns tax heavily, hoard commercial ratables, and lavish their schools with resources. [_Continue Reading_](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=a766d20ffa&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
|
---
|
# **3\. Lightning Round**
* [_From the Editors: It’s time to end the gerrymandering wars_](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=d73edf5bc9&e=b7b3eddce2)
* [_Ben Mannes: Trump’s crackdown defies critics as crime rates drop_](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=1c10021641&e=b7b3eddce2)
* [_Rep. Abby Major: Shapiro promised transparency. Instead, Pennsylvanians got secrecy._](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=a786485e5f&e=b7b3eddce2)
* [_Sen. Tracy Pennycuick: A no-new-tax-increase budget moves Pennsylvania forward_](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=c30d2a3201&e=b7b3eddce2)
* [_Christine Flowers: When I’m 64_](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=310539cca3&e=b7b3eddce2)
* [_Paul Davis: Beware of fake U.S. Postal Service emails during the holiday season_](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=171b0105cf&e=b7b3eddce2)
* [_Stew Bolno: Doing the opposite — George Costanza and Donald Trump_](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=956eb047b2&e=b7b3eddce2)
* [_Jeff Hurvitz: Kennedy should follow the science on vaccines_](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=0556437df7&e=b7b3eddce2)

---
|
---
|
# **4\. What we're reading. **
The Covid-era scam around the Minnesota non-profit, “Feeding Our Future” was already known to the world when Kamala Harris chose that state’s governor, Tim Walz, over Pennsylvania’s Josh Shapiro to be her vice-presidential running mate in 2024. But this week, the size and scope of the theft by organizations and people within Minnesota’s Somali community has exploded across the headlines of even those organizations that would normally be loath to tarnish a progressive Democrat’s reputation.
The [_New York Times_ _reports that_](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=6f214d7b8f&e=b7b3eddce2) “more than $1 billion in taxpayers’ money has been stolen in three plots they are investigating,” according to federal officials. There was a lot of scammy activity during the rollout of Covid relief funds, but Minnesota is showing itself to have been head-and-shoulders above the rest in corruption. The scale was so great that we have to ask ourselves whether Walz knew about it and ignored it or was too dumb to pick up on it — either answer is bad and makes you wonder what Harris ever saw in this guy.
---
|
# **5\. Correction**
_In last week’s edition of the Weekly Reads, we inadvertently credited an article to Todd Shepherd. The actual author was Guy Ciarrocchi. We regret the error._
---
|
---

Hope you enjoy!
The book is now available for [pre-sale](https://substack.com/redirect/ad381bcf-cd09-493a-aa35-35b85c08794d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and I hope you consider buying a copy. Use code "UBIUBI" for 25% off.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/da4f3757-ac38-4579-920d-bc17f771b556?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**Chapter 3 - How It Started**
It's at least a little necessary to go into my background. Plus, some of it's funny.
I was born in 1975 in Schenectady, New York. My parents were immigrants from Taiwan who met in college. Dad was a physicist for GE. His specialty was making screens flatter. He would jump up and down on them. Kidding, he worked in a lab. He grew up on a peanut farm in southern Taiwan with no floor, so he's a bit of a roughneck. He spits a lot. He swam in the local pond and came back from college to do farm work during the summers. He got out of Taiwan by doing well on a test and studying physics.
He hated the cold. Why was he in upstate New York? Because GE was the only place that would hire him. He wasn't getting offers because his PhD advisor wasn't a fan. But his advisor was away when GE was hiring. So they shrugged and made him an offer. He later moved to IBM a few hours south in part to escape the cold.
Dad was a pro wrestling fan. We would watch WWE together, and he would narrate like the matches were real. "Rabish Rick is too powerful for him. Look at that move. So devastating." Even I knew that they were staged.
When I was twelve or so, Dad hit a deer with his Ford Escort. The deer was dead, but Dad thought that it was a waste of food, so he took the deer, tied it to the top of the car, and drove to a butcher to get it chopped up. I came home to a freezer of frozen venison. My Dad never got the car repaired because he thought it was a waste of money, so the dented car was a permanent reminder. The deer didn't taste great.
Another time my Mom had a couple friends over. Dad didn't want to socialize so he took off. He wasn't an attentive driver. That time he backed into my Mom's friend's car in the driveway and then drove off without saying a word. She had to come back in the house and say, "Excuse me Nancy, but I think your husband rear-ended my car." My Mom was mortified. It was the lamest hit-and-run ever. Insert Asian driving joke here.
When Dad wanted to take us for a treat we would head to McDonald's. We would pour out two large fries into a pile in the middle of the tray and snap them up while eating our McNuggets or Big Macs. What we didn't notice for quite a while was that Dad had a habit of tossing the end of the French fry he was eating back in the pile; I guess he didn't want to eat the part of the fry that he had touched. By the end of the meal there were a bunch of tiny fry ends that my brother and I unwittingly ate for years! When we realized what was happening we were furious.
My Mom is more or less an angel. She and Dad met at UC Berkeley, where she got a Bachelor's in Math and a Master's in Statistics. She's the glue to our family and the reason we talk to other people. She would shake her head every time she washed the dishes: "Hooo, when I was in college, my job was to wash test tubes in the lab. Your Dad said if I married him, I would never wash dishes again. So what do I do every night? Wash dishes." She took us to piano lessons and Chinese school and tennis camp. By day she was the Director of Computer Services at the local public university, SUNY Purchase. She gardens, arranges flowers and is now an award-winning pastel artist. (Seriously, check out [nancyyangart.com](https://substack.com/redirect/4e06b973-0bde-4fa3-b069-71c1fc8d5849?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Love you Mom!)
I was a very quiet, nerdy, sensitive kid. I got named "Most Pensive" in pre-school because I never talked. I read comic books and fantasy novels and played with action figures, Star Wars and GI Joe in particular. My older brother and I discovered Dungeons and Dragons and spent endless hours crafting characters and having adventures. My characters tended to take one of 3 forms: the suicidal warrior, the scarred magic-user, and the happy-go-lucky thief.
My parents didn't know what pajamas were. I went to sleep in whatever clothes I wore that day my entire childhood. It was only in middle school that I figured out that wasn't cool. I also regularly wore the same clothes multiple times a week, until another kid called me a 'dirtbag' and I had to figure out what they were talking about.
There was a "country day" in my middle school where we all brought in food from our parent's culture. Most kids brought in pasta or Italian sausages. I brought in a rice cooker of fried rice. The other kids freaked out - "You eat this at home? Your house is like a Chinese restaurant?" I didn't tell them about the "Vitasoy" soy milk or bean curd in the fridge.
My town, Somers, was 96% white, with a high concentration of Italians. I suppose you'd call them "Guidos" in 80s parlance. When Platoon came out in middle school, one of the kids kept standing next to me saying, "You see that? That's the way the gook laughs."
I was one of the only Asian kids in town. There was one other Chinese girl in town and everyone said we should date. We both hated it.
I got reminded of my race quite often. Here are some of the more memorable samples:
"What's up, chink."
"Hey Yang, how's the wang?"
"Hey . . . you . . . wanna fight?" said with mouth moving but no sound coming out, to imitate a kung fu movie with bad dubbing.
"Ching chong ching chong."
"Hey Yang, are you related to Long Duk Dong?"
"Hey, you know what Chinese use for blindfolds? Dental floss!"
"Hey, Yang, you hungry? You want a gook-ie?"
"Hey Yang, I see where you're looking. No interracial dating."
"Hey Yang, what's it like having such a small dick? Everyone knows Chinese guys have small dicks. Do you need tweezers to jerk off?"
Middle school kids are creative.
It didn't help that I had skipped a grade and was thus smaller and scrawnier than most of my classmates. I was the last kid picked in gym for most any team. I remember one day we were playing softball, and I got stuck in the outfield to minimize the chances of my getting involved in a play. Of course the ball got hit right to me. It was a pop-up fly ball, eminently catchable. It hung in the air for what felt like forever.
"Catch it! Catch it!"
I raised my glove to catch the ball. The sun hit my eyes. I squinted up. And . . .
The ball hit the heel of my glove and popped out.
"Ahhhh! Throw it, throw it!"
The batter was sprinting around the base paths. Another kid in the outfield who had come over grabbed the ball from the ground and threw it toward the infield. He then looked at me with a pained look on his face and shook his head.
Here's a pic to give you an idea of what we were working with.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e36f1d56-538d-4bc2-b733-1f42dfacc5f0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The braces were coming soon.
I had a few natural responses to the ridicule. I became quite self-conscious and alienated. I wondered if I did indeed have a small dick. There were many days I wanted to avoid school.
Last, I became very, very angry.
As you can tell, the book is a bit different for me and tells stories I never could before. I hope you check it out and pick up a copy! Here is a link to [pre-order](https://substack.com/redirect/ad381bcf-cd09-493a-aa35-35b85c08794d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg); use code "UBIUBI" for 25% off. Also, dates are up for my book [tour](https://substack.com/redirect/fb0dd3bc-e825-4279-a1ff-c963336ffe00?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) next February so maybe I will see you in person soon to sign this bad boy. Hope your holidays are going great.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/1f0c8b9f-6544-43eb-99fd-8c1ad9352069?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
