* * *
# [The Economic Signals Everyone Should Pay Attention to in 2026](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=181233593&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxMjMzNTkzLCJpYXQiOjE3NjUzNzM2NzEsImV4cCI6MTc2Nzk2NTY3MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.j8NMkSP30EGiNoKjPo2YEziYrp2N-5Pr_OhqJNwYoxI)
### What Nvidia, OpenAI, job cuts, the Fed, and geopolitics tell us about 2026 (and why operators win the next cycle)
| | [Patrick J. Murphy](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---
| Dec 10
---
| [](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=181233593&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxMjMzNTkzLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2NTM3MzY3MSwiZXhwIjoxNzY3OTY1NjcxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDM5OTEzMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Bxj1xm2EabhmsrTA4XXXk9qYQ_dUMyhfvwgJ4oPsrsc&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=181233593&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxMjMzNTkzLCJpYXQiOjE3NjUzNzM2NzEsImV4cCI6MTc2Nzk2NTY3MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.j8NMkSP30EGiNoKjPo2YEziYrp2N-5Pr_OhqJNwYoxI&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=181233593&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxMjMzNTkzLCJpYXQiOjE3NjUzNzM2NzEsImV4cCI6MTc2Nzk2NTY3MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.j8NMkSP30EGiNoKjPo2YEziYrp2N-5Pr_OhqJNwYoxI)
---
| | [](https://substack..zt16HhrQ94zFESX1rgLaVpvEuwRSaNnEkHa-X44HxME?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/patrickmurphypa/p/the-economic-signals-everyone-should?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Nothing about this year behaved the way the textbooks said it should. Interest rates fell while the job market remained sluggish. Tech stocks soared while small businesses filed for bankruptcy at the fastest pace in the six-year history of Subchapter V.

Below are the signals from 2025 that could shape 2026.
# **The Great AI Race: Nvidia vs Google**
The year belonged to Nvidia, the pick-and-shovels king of AI's gold rush. Every major AI breakthrough, from ChatGPT to Google's Gemini runs on Nvidia's GPU hardware. You can have the smartest AI models in the world, but without compute, you have nothing. That's why Nvidia's stock hit all-time highs with a market cap closing in on [$5 trillion.](https://substack.com/redirect/50cf3dc6-d0e7-4ab8-8a59-a21e74a0d2f7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Google spent 2025 trying to claw its way back, launching Gemini Ultra 3 and reorganizing internally. Apple has also positioned itself for an on-device AI launch.

But the most prosperous outcome of 2026 won't be determined by who has the best model. It will be determined by who controls the most electricity, the most data centers, and the most supply.

**Nvidia controls all of this:**
\- the dominant GPU architecture (CUDA)
\- the H100 / H200 supply chain
\- key networking components (InfiniBand)
\- AI server design (DGX systems)
Are we approaching the edge of an AI bubble?
You can argue both sides.
There is a case that spending has outpaced revenue. Infrastructure build-outs have run ahead of monetization. Too many companies have attached themselves to AI to justify valuations - even if it's just smoke and mirrors.
There is also a case that productivity gains are real and enterprise adoption is deeper than the public narrative suggests.
My fellow investors separate the wheat from the chaff, the hype cycles versus real capability, but digging in and using our own experience with technologies that were misunderstood years ago.
What most people miss is this. Bubbles do not form around the technology. They form around misplaced hype. They form when execution cannot support expectations. It's why I surround myself with wicked smart, hard-working and honest people.
The companies with strong operator cultures will navigate 2026. The companies with misaligned leadership teams will not.
## **The New Gold Rush: Data Centers**
AI isn't just a technology story; it's a land & energy story.
America is seeing the closest thing to a modern gold rush in data center construction. Construction workers, electricians, HVAC technicians, and steel fabricators are being swallowed into projects backed by hyperscalers, sovereign wealth funds, and private equity.

We talk about data centers like they're real estate plays. Before the Pentagon, when I served in Congress on the appropriations energy & water subcommittee, this wasn't even a concept. But the resource that actually constrains them is electricity. The U.S. grid was not built for clusters of compute farms drinking power like oil rigs in Houston.
In 2025, multiple states froze data center proposals because they couldn't guarantee supply. Utilities in the Midwest reported decade-high demand spikes. Transmission bottlenecks became national security concerns.
By 2026, the battle won't be over AI models--it will be over megawatts. Corporations will bid for power the way sovereign nations bid for oil. The economy's limiting reagent is no longer venture capital. It's electrons - and in 2025 that [electricity costs American households 11% more.](https://substack.com/redirect/b57dea6c-d5d8-4827-8572-1c77c4eca533?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
In some ways, it mirrors the internet boom of the 1990s, only this time the winners are cities with cheap land, megawatt capacity, and flexible zoning.

Data centers in the Midwest. EV manufacturing in the Southeast. AI hubs in Phoenix, Pittsburgh, and Austin. 2026 could be the first year where the fastest-growing economic corridor in the country is not coastal at all.
# **The November Jobs Shock: Private-Sector Layoffs**
The [November ADP report](https://substack.com/redirect/191bb1ee-15c1-4652-bfa1-564e6fd1cbb9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) gave us the clearest signal yet that the labor market has entered its slow-bleed phase: private-sector payrolls fell by 32,000 jobs, almost entirely because small businesses shed 120,000 workers -- their steepest monthly decline since the pandemic.

That brings the total planned cuts for the year to [1.17 million](https://substack.com/redirect/d1d7f69d-442b-4e89-b85e-183e4c57d629?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- the highest year-to-date level of job losses since 2020.
Hiring plans are even weaker: employers have announced fewer than 500,000 intended hires this year, the lowest level since 2010, and seasonal hiring is at a record low.
# **Mergers and acquisitions**
The Monday after Thanksgiving, two of the biggest advertising holding companies in the world--[Omnicom and IPG](https://substack.com/redirect/5b2fd36e-0c0d-46d4-8d43-d44bf39f895e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--announced they were merging and cut 4,000 jobs on the spot.

By the end of the week, Netflix announced it was acquiring Warner Bros. Discovery for $82.7 billion.

[The Writers Guild of America](https://substack.com/redirect/93750bf4-9e9b-4cf5-940c-bebb6e1248a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) released a statement railing against the merger.
> The outcome would eliminate jobs, push down wages, worsen conditions for all entertainment workers, raise prices for consumers, and reduce the volume and diversity of content for all viewers. Industry workers along with the public are already impacted by only a few powerful companies maintaining tight control over what consumers can watch on television, on streaming, and in theaters. This merger must be blocked.
# **The Robotaxi Tipping Point: Waymo and China Pull Ahead**
Remember when self-driving cars were a running joke?

Then 2025 happened.

Waymo's fleets in Phoenix and San Francisco logged milestones analysts didn't expect until 2030. China--unburdened by regulation--approved robotaxi operations across multiple provinces.
Pretty soon, robotaxis won't be a novelty; they'll be a labor shock.
They will reshape insurance, real estate, commuting patterns, and even municipal budgets.
2026 will be the first year autonomous mobility enters mainstream American life.
## **Retail spending is down**
Chipotle's slowdown is a macro story about consumer spending. The chain said its younger customers--especially those earning under $100,000--are coming in less often, squeezed by unemployment, student loan payments, and wages that haven't kept up with their costs. This group, which usually accounts for [40% of Chipotle's sales](https://substack.com/redirect/d94305e8-88e6-4b79-be1a-485afae15128?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), isn't defecting to competitors; they're cooking at home. Same-store sales flattened, and the stock has slid more than 30% over the last year. Inflation might be cooling on paper, but in practice, people are trimming the small luxuries first. When Chipotle loses momentum with 25- to 35-year-olds, the broader consumer picture is already shifting.

[Gen-Z is not spending money.](https://substack.com/redirect/4d44aead-509c-4e13-a28b-d98cdd432097?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

Inflation technically remained flat. Core CPI (Consumer Price Index) rose 3% year-over-year, down from its 2022 peak of 6.6% but higher than last year's 2.9%. But once a price goes up, without wages keeping pace, the increase calcifies. A lower inflation rate doesn't make groceries cheaper; it just means the price stops rising so quickly.
#
**Americans are Drowning in Debt**
The number is staggering: **[$18.59 trillion](https://substack.com/redirect/6dbbc1fd-ca2f-4f44-84b0-948613a4b272?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[ in household debt.](https://substack.com/redirect/6dbbc1fd-ca2f-4f44-84b0-948613a4b272?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (Federal debt rose to [$38.4 trillion](https://substack.com/redirect/ca10425e-7baa-4560-857a-0309e5e08ebb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))
Everything is up:
* Mortgages
* Auto loans
* Student loans
* HELOCs
* Credit cards

Prices went up.
Wages did not.

Young people sense something isn't right and many are pissed that they'll be responsible for paying off the $38.4 trillion and growing national debt, while decades of unpaid-for tax cuts at the top keep shrinking the government's ability to invest in anything that helps them.

When the wealthiest were taxed more on the top end of their income, it became less attractive for executives to demand outsized pay packages -- a very different world from today, where we're debating whether Elon Musk should receive a compensation package valued at more than a trillion dollars. Companies were more likely to reinvest in workers and long-term growth. And government revenue funded enormous public projects, from the GI Bill to highways to NASA.
Today, the top rate is 37% for single filers earning about $626,000 after deductions. And instead of investing in national capacity, the U.S. now spends more than $1 trillion a year just servicing interest on the debt. It's a very different fiscal landscape, and younger generations know exactly who's going to be left holding the bill.
# **The Dollar Store Economy**
One of the strangest data points of late 2025 came from the aisles between discount dish soap and off-brand cereal. Dollar stores always get busy when the economy is struggling. They did during the [Great Recession](https://substack.com/redirect/29f31166-5e63-4b6f-baa5-8c7594de169e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). They did during the [early pandemic](https://substack.com/redirect/4756c33c-db3c-4041-ad1e-155ef6309642?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). And they're doing it again now, only this time the customer base is broader and a lot more affluent.

[Dollar Tree](https://substack.com/redirect/5f521034-741b-4ce7-9f0b-1f47659df7ab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) announced that **60% of its new shoppers now earn more than $100,000 a year**. Dollar General said something similar: higher-income households are suddenly walking through the door in meaningful numbers.

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/482391b9-ab39-4125-b60f-d5aeabdd42ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
A Dollar General store in Germantown, New York._Photographer: Angus Mordant/Bloomberg_
These chains were in a slump not long ago. When inflation peaked and people redirected their money toward travel and experiences, dollar stores struggled.

That flipped this fall.
[Dollar General](https://substack.com/redirect/964460e7-3cf0-4a92-aeec-c12c40f5ee86?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) raised its full-year profit outlook after a quarter that beat expectations across the board--sales, earnings, foot traffic. The stock jumped 12% in a single day. Dollar Tree did the same the day before. Five Below blew past expectations too; its same-store sales jumped 14%, almost double Wall Street's estimates.
### **
The Tariff Shock Is Coming**
Expect:
* Tariffs on EVs, steel, and chips
* Retaliatory tariffs from China
* Higher prices on everything from appliances to apparel

## **Good News: Markets Hit Record Highs Because of Seven Companies**
The [S&P 500](https://substack.com/redirect/4c8a8e56-a8b4-49fe-85cf-4e52c70cb201?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is approaching a new record high and the Nasdaq has delivered steady year-end numbers, but that stability was built on the strength of just seven companies. Nvidia alone added more market cap than most countries generate in GDP. It was the defining stock of 2025 and the gravitational force behind the indices.
By Q4, the "magnificent seven" were driving nearly 40 percent of index performance.
While more than 60% of Americans invest or have their retirements tied to the market, this is good news - but there is concentration risk in how overreliance on a handful of players could shift its performance quickly.
#
**Geopolitical Pressure Was Constant, Yet Markets Absorbed It With Unusual Speed**
From U.S.-China tech tensions to instability in the Middle East, from regulatory swings after the election to supply-chain friction that still had not fully normalized, geopolitical pressure remained the baseline of 2025.

New rounds of tariffs landed right as global supply chains were trying to stabilize, raising prices for manufacturers and stirring fears of slower growth. Markets reacted the way they always do when policy uncertainty meets soft demand: they pulled back.
But something interesting happened. Markets absorbed shocks faster than analysts expected and reached record highs. The Federal Reserve signaled rate cuts earlier than expected and inflation remained flat.
Not because conditions improved. They didn't.
Markets simply adapted to these geopolitical challenges, especially when AI earnings blew past expectations.

Again, operators at these top companies became the differentiator.
#
**Are We Actually Headed for a Soft Landing?**
Through 2025, the Federal Reserve kept rates at their highest level in two decades.

Inflation has remained steady at roughly 2.9% -- a long way from the 6-9% peaks of 2022, but still above the Fed's comfort zone.

The third straight rate cut this year will likely be announced at today's FOMC meeting, marking the end of the tightening cycle.

Monetary policy can prepare the runway. It cannot guarantee the landing.
The Fed keeps talking about a "soft landing" while the underlying numbers tell a messier story.

The real landing will be determined by leadership inside companies, not just policymakers. The Fed can shape the macro environment, but operators will determine the micro outcomes for their teams, shareholders, and stakeholders.

**So What Does All This Mean for 2026?**
Expect a year defined by volatility: markets that swing on every inflation print, sectors that rise and fall based on AI infrastructure bottlenecks, and monthly jobs reports that matter more than they have in a decade.

Next week, we'll give insight into the current geopolitical landscape, including risks, resiliency, and pressure points that will shape America's economic and security outlook in 2026.
Thank you for reading. If my writing resonates with you, please consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.

What Nvidia, OpenAI, job cuts, the Fed, and geopolitics tell us about 2026 (and why operators win the next cycle) | | | Dec 10 | | | | | | | | | | | | Nothing about this year behaved the way the textbooks said it should. Interest rates fell while the job market remained sluggish. Tech stocks soared while small businesses filed for bankruptcy at the fastest pace in the six-year history of Subchapter V. Below are the signals from 2025 that could shape 2026. The Great AI Race: Nvidia vs Google The year belonged to Nvidia, the pick-and-shovels king of AI's gold rush. Every major AI breakthrough, from ChatGPT to Google's Gemini runs on Nvidia's GPU hardware. You can have the smartest AI models in the world, but without compute, you have nothing. That's why Nvidia's stock hit all-time highs with a market cap closing in on Google spent 2025 trying to claw its way back, launching Gemini Ultra 3 and reorganizing internally. Apple has also positioned itself for an on-device AI launch. But the most prosperous outcome of 2026 won't be determined by who has the best model. It will be determined by who controls the most electricity, the most data centers, and the most supply. Nvidia controls all of this: \- the dominant GPU architecture (CUDA) \- the H100 / H200 supply chain \- key networking components (InfiniBand) \- AI server design (DGX systems) Are we approaching the edge of an AI bubble? You can argue both sides. There is a case that spending has outpaced revenue. Infrastructure build-outs have run ahead of monetization. Too many companies have attached themselves to AI to justify valuations - even if it's just smoke and mirrors. There is also a case that productivity gains are real and enterprise adoption is deeper than the public narrative suggests. My fellow investors separate the wheat from the chaff, the hype cycles versus real capability, but digging in and using our own experience with technologies that were misunderstood years ago. What most people miss is this. Bubbles do not form around the technology. They form around misplaced hype. They form when execution cannot support expectations. It's why I surround myself with wicked smart, hard-working and honest people. The companies with strong operator cultures will navigate 2026. The companies with misaligned leadership teams will not. The New Gold Rush: Data Centers AI isn't just a technology story; it's a land & energy story. America is seeing the closest thing to a modern gold rush in data center construction. Construction workers, electricians, HVAC technicians, and steel fabricators are being swallowed into projects backed by hyperscalers, sovereign wealth funds, and private equity. We talk about data centers like they're real estate plays. Before the Pentagon, when I served in Congress on the appropriations energy & water subcommittee, this wasn't even a concept. But the resource that actually constrains them is electricity. The U.S. grid was not built for clusters of compute farms drinking power like oil rigs in Houston. In 2025, multiple states froze data center proposals because they couldn't guarantee supply. Utilities in the Midwest reported decade-high demand spikes. Transmission bottlenecks became national security concerns. By 2026, the battle won't be over AI models--it will be over megawatts. Corporations will bid for power the way sovereign nations bid for oil. The economy's limiting reagent is no longer venture capital. It's electrons - and in 2025 that In some ways, it mirrors the internet boom of the 1990s, only this time the winners are cities with cheap land, megawatt capacity, and flexible zoning. Data centers in the Midwest. EV manufacturing in the Southeast. AI hubs in Phoenix, Pittsburgh, and Austin. 2026 could be the first year where the fastest-growing economic corridor in the country is not coastal at all. body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120977731/587965127/1895761198?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZjZiYTA4Ny1kMGQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8tKqxcixWZBfI4QIVNmart6_5ybWggKZ3Fg9Al1COho=&emci=6e4579e3-22d5-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=6f6ba087-d0d5-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=13185894) Derek, last week I spoke with MS NOW about the incompetent leadership of Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth: [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120977739/587965142/320240526?refcode=email_121025_hegseth&amounts=5,15,25,50,100&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZjZiYTA4Ny1kMGQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8tKqxcixWZBfI4QIVNmart6_5ybWggKZ3Fg9Al1COho=) **Pete Hegseth is a failure, Derek.** I don’t think that’s a controversial opinion. He shared sensitive, classified information about a U.S. military strike in a Signal group chat. He put pilots and operational success at risk. He has failed us and our military in so many ways. The Pentagon Inspector General agrees, saying Hegseth’s “actions created a risk to operational security that could have resulted in failed US mission objectives and potential harm to US pilots.” **This is amateur stuff. It's a threat to our military, and it’s unacceptable.** We need the best person possible leading our military. There are nearly 350 million Americans: Pete Hegseth is not the best person in America to be the Secretary of Defense. [**I’m taking on Pete Hegseth and his reckless leadership and protecting our military every chance I get. body:
They come for me because I fight for YOU.
These emails are the best way for our campaign to stay in touch with supporters like you. If you'd prefer, you can [unsubscribe here](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/K-_-4uI0EkWyg9pVsZKBKQ2?unsubscribedata=7VQA1BXoUlPICDtlj1%2B2%2BN9Cy8ItluEG5CtvutY6pN%2FUXwURWU4cbfsfgNXLR%2BKX9zWl6SGnpfpGCz34LPOVZPA5k1z%2F6DXxjnVAAhlx%2FMJxlVoiK67DeVAtok3Xyx%2B3Tpae4oCnN%2B4Ov90QBbucnZrmC61r%2FNf8ychcDUxFMlI8yqNNfXRdCm%2FCIWFoQGk%2BiIlduez%2BuzBLgBZjWWKvPsTBSFq2zJgc1VpQuQD1oBKbYoJLrkUi0CzNMa8SEKnoVzY6%2B86aM0gRwtCnd94Y7%2Fx0ybQkn1SMwvE%2FdXwwouhzlJYHTh6iuC0VYkHw8b5XA6dzUh31fdLP4PSgFcyhHO3oTJ9Pq8vMygiq%2Fqj0pj1HDdZfeGFUEvhf2gaF%2FQdHMx6JnHvwYsKLRBX%2BI6DEViZshmlz%2F9x%2Fdq2mc5UZNoOKxEqjNCq%2BmLjYh47yNAnN).
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120980315/587974176/-652557664?amount=25&amounts=250,100,50,25,10,5&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.10_Crockett-Split-Epstein_B1_F1_S1_C1_30CL-365DON_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MzE2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMmRkNWIzYS1kMWQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=i0O7La-QVsKnbwMry8hnJft5kMWApbbLTBhFXsRTsDI=) Peter, Let me be very clear about something: I don’t back down from a fight. Not when Donald Trump attacks me.
Not when extremists try to silence me.
Not when bullies threaten my office, my staff, or my community. **If you’ve followed the news, you already know exactly what I mean:** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120980316/587974177/-652557664?amount=25&amounts=250,100,50,25,10,5&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.10_Crockett-Split-Epstein_B1_F1_S1_C1_30CL-365DON_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MzE2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMmRkNWIzYS1kMWQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=i0O7La-QVsKnbwMry8hnJft5kMWApbbLTBhFXsRTsDI=) | | [ DONATE NOW → ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120980317/587974178/-652557664?amount=25&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,250&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.10_Crockett-Split-Epstein_B1_F1_S1_C1_30CL-365DON_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MzE2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMmRkNWIzYS1kMWQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=i0O7La-QVsKnbwMry8hnJft5kMWApbbLTBhFXsRTsDI=)

They’ve thrown everything they can at me — insults, smear campaigns, threats — all because I tell the truth. All because I refuse to let corruption, white supremacy, or criminality go unchecked. All because I will always, always, stand up to a bully.
But here’s what those headlines don’t show:
Every time they come at me, _**I get more determined.**_
Every time they try to intimidate me, _**I fight harder.**_
Every time someone tells me to sit down and be quiet, _**I stand taller and speak louder.**_
Because I’m not fighting for myself — I’m fighting for the people who deserve a government that actually works for them.
**And now, I’m taking that fight to the United States Senate.**
Texas deserves a Senator who won’t fold under pressure, who will call out injustice wherever it shows up, and who isn’t afraid of Donald Trump or the MAGA Movement.
**That’s why I need this team with me from the very start. This race will be brutal. They will lie, spend, and smear without hesitation. But if we show our strength early — if we show them that this movement doesn’t scare easily — we can win this.**

clean_body: They come for me because I fight for YOU. These emails are the best way for our campaign to stay in touch with supporters like you. If you'd prefer, you can . = Peter, Let me be very clear about something: I don’t back down from a fight. Not when Donald Trump attacks me. Not when extremists try to silence me. Not when bullies threaten my office, my staff, or my community. If you’ve followed the news, you already know exactly what I mean: = | | = They’ve thrown everything they can at me — insults, smear campaigns, threats — all because I tell the truth. All because I refuse to let corruption, white supremacy, or criminality go unchecked. All because I will always, always, stand up to a bully. But here’s what those headlines don’t show: Every time they come at me, I get more determined. Every time they try to intimidate me, I fight harder. Every time someone tells me to sit down and be quiet, I stand taller and speak louder. Because I’m not fighting for myself — I’m fighting for the people who deserve a government that actually works for them. And now, I’m taking that fight to the United States Senate. Texas deserves a Senator who won’t fold under pressure, who will call out injustice wherever it shows up, and who isn’t afraid of Donald Trump or the MAGA Movement. That’s why I need this team with me from the very start. This race will be brutal. They will lie, spend, and smear without hesitation. But if we show our strength early — if we show them that this movement doesn’t scare easily — we can win this. = | = | = | = | = | = | = Texas is worth fighting for. Democracy is worth fighting for. And I’m ready — as always — to fight like hell. Are you? Jasmine Crockett | These emails are the best way for our campaign to stay in touch with supporters like you. If you'd prefer to receive fewer emails, click here. If you'd prefer, you can . If you'd like to donate, =. body:
We just took a trip to the outskirts of Memphis.| | | [](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=dac9be552b394676b24089d13145d082&_e=bHFZX6TgYtOax_GhbAgkIDPgAmMUOgrIye_1QrcsSOMKagBp27rqXfFLsI_ki9_l2HqTG2cMUNGAeIPzSTgqmTTtY44dF9vFDl4Rr7j4vqsJcUen3IMqhRi-JxZTZ6gCmm0Qa2e-FmKRPUNvC2PRSCUt1vsxnuSmI0t--ug_CmktK2SpxU6fJrOoLk6ZlvZC2qbOo-u-R7obUmgoHVR7zyGvXU9Bz1fBjKVcMhKFzEKRBHqUQuXWnZrLWWYNs-DjlteoOiCBu0i6ehSV0bnCQvyXVJ45S0Jq_PaKNoAoDTLxdz1lmUNj_jE6hzDE6mlj-Y-xjxyql4aDhY7biYKSBQ%3D%3D)

| | **Caught on Camera: What We Found In Memphis Shocked Us**
---
| [ Click Here ](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=dac9be552b394676b24089d13145d082&_e=bHFZX6TgYtOax_GhbAgkIBxPMQI1e_BiNmJC3T323mKLh5dVzZ6WXEl9e4kCzVGf-SQLzTieiQMCI34SVfF6KqKs6igJLrqusqlLYczKowWJ-lWJQTEvpSd5gYbqFV4ZU0cqpH_aq_ihDF6J7hGqmTGDrPAHOinF2nrZChmhmCEu_ExpS7Q9A6IFXXD7s8tNZ7Npzr7o8Y9lUCTuwFIWP3U1OjpxnTP0_6HgQ5Nk0L1HknTFr_0NRuhbwKu8LOKar71ZvER0nIceh4FQLaFW0gOBzqm0ZCy2CRxTnVEK8yuHoKTAOX01pW6YJPSqW1IQfdeYcgMp5881jERswjAvSSNqFChaPNHWkLBxZtjoCRvqG8SYEgpMbI1lJIXxVFL9CoxMrMIrmq_acYSmBqElQh1LwCBGmINVUqb47ZWok36GdfWOSKoyltgouMUABWBX4D3nBY8l1Zc4ItUaTSKswLcgC-cV3jloWw9pueJFM9KNyp7koyiAEZpZmnhZ-TFeY3z1eujyiCIxst2aOOiXl85SuoWqHH7cmM_cbAgcWuZT0GdJCE66LFo_QUMkW7SbQRP970ZT9HFv64H5TigCcg%3D%3D)
---
| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

We just took a trip to the outskirts of Memphis.
We brought the cameras.

This wasn’t a normal data center.
It’s the kind of build that could redefine AI—
and maybe destroy what’s around it.

We had to see it with our own eyes.
Now, we want you to see it too.

[_Click here to watch what’s happening in Memphis before it disappears._](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=dac9be552b394676b24089d13145d082&_e=bHFZX6TgYtOax_GhbAgkIBxPMQI1e_BiNmJC3T323mKLh5dVzZ6WXEl9e4kCzVGf-SQLzTieiQMCI34SVfF6KqKs6igJLrqusqlLYczKowWJ-lWJQTEvpSd5gYbqFV4ZU0cqpH_aq_ihDF6J7hGqmTGDrPAHOinF2nrZChmhmCEu_ExpS7Q9A6IFXXD7s8tN-1HlwTnr_1OgzeeOKbs-1w7zVFTkD1uxUEXBeJ1ruhuxMbHTD4VnUtBqmmzDb9ZErph9vNXOrW_cj3iUsQFf4-HuB0WXfgxzIptMPXAR5OH2YzWm5tmoSijtwj5tarNy9obvHMpPS1HamxOuOdD8AZN5dITgqzlBtT6H7P2EsVdvgvEAtzuvsvyceSUdwNgilfoQzMe-Ymlj7_1mOdU-6XTiUDAjmvPJBc9i4TGJu6UlE-7So76Nh57qKX8nyN_CcViHT6aFD-nSRPaKfWIdSPjv9Kd90kCwtbQ9VOS9JCazF8FiXylpdY6G6NHa4DSKwAwQDAN-i_RWFHKlfndzKpM4nco_D3tFI8C6BXRYJHGh2F9FjVNotoufjQSqgPKgnb4ujJ8VEskTlymcaV1qNg%3D%3D)

**Behind the Markets**
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: We just took a trip to the outskirts of Memphis.| | | | December 10 | | | | | Caught on Camera: What We Found In Memphis Shocked Us | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. It’s the kind of build that could redefine AI— and maybe destroy what’s around it. We had to see it with our own eyes. Now, we want you to see it too. All the best, Simmy Adelman, Publisher Behind the Markets Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. Fellow Patriot,

As this year winds down, I've been thinking about what it actually takes to win the fights ahead. Most people focus on Election Day, but the truth is that 2026 will be shaped by the groundwork we lay long before the campaign season ramps up.

The radical left isn't waiting. They're already organizing, fundraising, and mapping out where they think they can break through next cycle. If we want to stay ahead of them, we need to match that level of preparation - _not in six months, but right now._

**That's why these final end-of-year numbers matter so much.** They set the tone for 2026. They tell our opponents whether we're entering the new year confident and well-positioned, or if they should view us as vulnerable. I want us on offense from day one.

clean_body: Final end-of-year numbers matter so much. They set the tone for 2026. | Fellow Patriot, As this year winds down, I've been thinking about what it actually takes to win the fights ahead. Most people focus on Election Day, but the truth is that 2026 will be shaped by the groundwork we lay long before the campaign season ramps up. The radical left isn't waiting. They're already organizing, fundraising, and mapping out where they think they can break through next cycle. If we want to stay ahead of them, we need to match that level of preparation - not in six months, but right now. That's why these final end-of-year numbers matter so much. They set the tone for 2026. They tell our opponents whether we're entering the new year confident and well-positioned, or if they should view us as vulnerable. I want us on offense from day one. Thank you for standing with me. We have important work ahead - and it starts with how we finish this year. Bernie Moreno FOLLOW BERNIE ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | Paid for by Bernie Moreno for Senate Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go: .

name: Kathryn Harvey
subject: 🗳️Turn the lights out on Lee Bright December 23!
body:
📣Support Steve Evered for SC Senate tonight in Converse Heights.

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/61d88e4c775c/kathryn-harvey-for-congresscampaign-kickoff-6423226?e=edbde6c9b1)

|
---
| | Welcome to Keeping Up with Kathryn Harvey. A weekly digest covering South Carolina politics and opportunities for engagement and action in the Upstate.
---
| |
# We have a special election for Senate 12 on December 23, and we can WIN.
---
| |

Steve Evered is challenging the most extreme Senator the Upstate has seen in recent memory, [Lee Bright,](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=505300e0b8&e=edbde6c9b1) in the December 23 Special Election for Senate 12 as a write-in candidate. Senate 12 covers West Spartanburg, Reidville, Greer, East Greenville, Lyman and more. Lee Bright won the Republican Primary in October with 2500 votes. Steve is the former VP of Government Affairs for Michelin and he has come out of retirement to beat back Lee Bright who served from 2008-2016. Lee Bright fought to keep the confederate flag at the state house, sponsored extreme anti-abortion bills without exceptions, and advocated against the Affordable Care Act. We can’t afford for him to go back down to Columbia and expand the Freedom Caucus.
**You can learn more about Steve and his campaign here**[**https://www.everedforsc.com/**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=7ae39872e7&e=edbde6c9b1)** & you can join us TONIGHT in Converse to meet Steve and hear more about his campaign. **[**RSVP Here**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=eec04b135f&e=edbde6c9b1)
---
[|
---](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=35479c4072&e=edbde6c9b1)
| [Support Steve ](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=7ba41e4825&e=edbde6c9b1)
---
|
---
| |
# Four chances to meet Steve before election day!
---
| |
We have several opportunities to meet Steve and learn how you can support his race over the next two weeks. You can sign up to [phone bank here ](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=1b8f3f26d9&e=edbde6c9b1)or [knocks doors with us every day](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=a33e7cea27&e=edbde6c9b1).
[**Wednesday, December 10 at 5:30pm in Spartanburg’s Converse Heights**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=87cba5f825&e=edbde6c9b1)[**Thursday, December 11 at 6:00pm in Greenville at Project Host**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=1c79e9cd97&e=edbde6c9b1)[**Sunday, December 14 at 5:00pm in Spartanburg on the Westside**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=fdde9a4564&e=edbde6c9b1)[**Thursday, December 18 at 6:00pm at the Rights & Empowerment Art Show **](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=2d88ebf278&e=edbde6c9b1)

|

# Trump launches affordability tour
---
| |

[Trump mocks affordability, doubles down on tariffs and claims voters should give up certain products like pencils.](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=759010a126&e=edbde6c9b1)[Nancy Mace Op-ed on frustration with Republican Party & Congress](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=c008f6b073&e=edbde6c9b1)

| | | | |
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| | | | | | | | | | |

| [|
---](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=a951f9458c&e=edbde6c9b1)
| |
Last Monday we hosted candidates from across the state who are running in 2026 including US Senate, Governor, Superintendent of Education and Congress. We celebrated 2025 wins and launched Steve’s campaign for SC Senate 12!
Councilwoman Meghan Smith and I knocked doors in her district with Steve this past weekend and folks are getting excited about the Dec 23rd election. We visited with the Greenville Dem Women at their annual Holiday Party and they are fired up as well.
And finally, my good friend Tina Belge was sworn in to the Greenville City Council on Monday and they were able to hold their democratic majority because of her win. Save the date for January 27 when Octavia is sworn in for Commissioner of Public Works here in Spartanburg!
Democracy is a participatory sport, stay with me y’all.

name: Mark Kelly
subject: Following up on something my team asked you
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
I’m reaching out one more time this morning, before whatever happens with this report, to see if you’ll add your name alongside the others who signed on to have my back.
#

Morning, my friend.
I’m reaching out one more time this morning, before whatever happens with this report, to see if you’ll add your name alongside the others who signed on to have my back.
I know it’s the holidays and there’s a ton going on, but it would mean a lot.
So, are you with me?
[**If you’re ready, use this link and add your personal endorsement.**](https://act.markkelly.com/signup/mek-em-dec25-endorse/?refcode=em251210-1-160&akid=6288%2E6688874%2E3BQABd)
**[JOIN MARK](https://act.markkelly.com/signup/mek-em-dec25-endorse/?refcode=em251210-1-160&akid=6288%2E6688874%2E3BQABd)**
Thanks,
MK

* * *

Team,
In just a minute, we're going to ask if you will add your name to the thousands of others who have personally endorsed Mark.
Please give us a chance to explain why that endorsement is so important right now.
The truth is, we've never faced something quite like this.
Mark has never had to deal with threats from the White House, the Department of Defense, or the FBI all at the same time. Trump is trying to make an example out of Mark. He is trying to use the power of the presidency to shut him up and force him into silence.
We can't let Trump win.
Mark has always been lucky to have a strong team behind him, and that's gotten him through some tough moments. This is something else entirely.
We need everyone reading this, every person who believes in Mark, everybody who understands just how perilous this moment really is, to add their name here and personally endorse Mark.
It might seem like a small act, but by the time you've read this far another person will have already added their name. By the end of the day, we're hoping that thousands of people will be signed on. Can we count on you?
[**Add your name and personally endorse Mark Kelly. He needs you with him.**](https://act.markkelly.com/signup/mek-em-dec25-endorse/?source=em251210-1-160&akid=6288%2E6688874%2E3BQABd)
**[SIGN ON NOW](https://act.markkelly.com/signup/mek-em-dec25-endorse/?source=em251210-1-160&akid=6288%2E6688874%2E3BQABd)**
Thank you,
Team Kelly

Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here](https://act.markkelly.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=1&akid=6288%2E6688874%2E3BQABd).
Mark Kelly was a Captain in the U.S. Navy and astronaut. Use of his military rank, job titles and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or Department of Defense and reference to NASA does not imply endorsement by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration.

[**Elizabeth Warren is calling for an END to the Electoral College now. Without 125,OOO signatures, ****we won 't be able to show Democrats in Congress there's a critical mass. Please sign the petition now:**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/70853?t=7&akid=23710%2E4449941%2E8aH1nj)
[SIGN NOW >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/70853?t=8&akid=23710%2E4449941%2E8aH1nj)
---
This petition is registered to you only.

Donald Trump only became President in 2016 because of the outdated Electoral College.

_Hillary Clinton won the popular vote by nearly 2.9 million. But the Electoral College overruled her popular vote victory._

**Now, Sen. Elizabeth Warren is urgently pleading with Democrats:**
We couldn't agree more. But now that Trump is in office, he and his Republican cronies will do everything in their power to uphold the Electoral College.

**So we need to act now -- because we must prevent unpopular, unqualified candidates from EVER becoming President again.**

[**_If every Democrat reading this signs our petition, we will reach our 125,OOO signature goal within minutes and gain traction for this urgent legislation in Congress. Please add your name immediately:_**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/70853?t=9&akid=23710%2E4449941%2E8aH1nj)
[SIGN NOW >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/70853?t=10&akid=23710%2E4449941%2E8aH1nj)

Thank you,

_Fight for Reform_

body:
This is my calling
The values that guide me are simple: protect families, defend faith, and safeguard freedom.

| | Hi Peter,

My name is **Victor Marx** -- a husband of 37 years, father of five, grandfather, Marine Corps veteran, former pastor, and humanitarian who has spent decades serving families and communities around the world. **_I am also the conservative in the race for Governor of Colorado._** I'm running because the challenges we're facing here reflect the challenges facing our entire nation. What happens in one state can strengthen -- _or weaken_ -- the future of America. From war zones to trafficking corridors to communities devastated by violence, **I 've led more than 150 high-risk missions focused on rescuing and restoring the innocent.** That work exists because **God carried me** through a childhood marked by trauma, instability, and hardship. Through His grace, I rebuilt my life -- and I've spent every day since fighting for those who can't fight for themselves. **_This campaign is no different._** It isn't powered by insiders or special interests. It's powered by people who still believe in Faith, Family, Freedom, and the promise of a stronger America. We're already building real momentum, and we need to keep it going. Over the years, I've been blessed to walk alongside men and women of deep conviction--people who don't just speak about change but embody it in the way they live. One of those rare leaders I held in the highest regard was my friend, the late **Charlie Kirk.** After Charlie passed, his wife Erika told me that Charlie would want me to run, and she encouraged me to throw my hat in the ring. While I've never considered myself a politician--_nor do I intend to become one_ --I reached a point where I couldn't ignore the reality that our communities are being failed. **I knew I had to do something. ** _Thankfully_ , I'm not stepping into this fight alone. The values that guide me are simple: **protect families, defend faith, safeguard freedom, and steward the natural beauty we 've been entrusted with. **These are not just Colorado values -- _they 're American values._ The struggles we face here -- crime, government overreach, attacks on faith, rising costs -- are the same challenges pressing on families across the country. Turning Colorado around sends a message far beyond our borders: **that courage still matters, that conviction still wins, and that ordinary Americans can reclaim their future.** I believe deeply in that mission. _I 've lived it my entire life._ And I'm grateful to have so many determined patriots standing alongside me as we work toward a safer, stronger, more united future. clean_body: This is my calling The values that guide me are simple: protect families, defend faith, and safeguard freedom. | | | | | Hi Peter, My name is Victor Marx -- a husband of 37 years, father of five, grandfather, Marine Corps veteran, former pastor, and humanitarian who has spent decades serving families and communities around the world. I am also the conservative in the race for Governor of Colorado. I'm running because the challenges we're facing here reflect the challenges facing our entire nation. What happens in one state can strengthen -- or weaken -- the future of America. For years, my calling has taken me into some of the most difficult places imaginable. From war zones to trafficking corridors to communities devastated by violence, I 've led more than 150 high-risk missions focused on rescuing and restoring the innocent. That work exists because God carried me through a childhood marked by trauma, instability, and hardship. Through His grace, I rebuilt my life -- and I've spent every day since fighting for those who can't fight for themselves. This campaign is no different. It isn't powered by insiders or special interests. It's powered by people who still believe in Faith, Family, Freedom, and the promise of a stronger America. We're already building real momentum, and we need to keep it going. I want you with us in this fight, Over the years, I've been blessed to walk alongside men and women of deep conviction--people who don't just speak about change but embody it in the way they live. One of those rare leaders I held in the highest regard was my friend, the late Charlie Kirk. After Charlie passed, his wife Erika told me that Charlie would want me to run, and she encouraged me to throw my hat in the ring. While I've never considered myself a politician--nor do I intend to become one --I reached a point where I couldn't ignore the reality that our communities are being failed. I knew I had to do something. Thankfully , I'm not stepping into this fight alone. Thousands of patriots have already taken their place in this movement, The values that guide me are simple: protect families, defend faith, safeguard freedom, and steward the natural beauty we 've been entrusted with. These are not just Colorado values -- they 're American values. The struggles we face here -- crime, government overreach, attacks on faith, rising costs -- are the same challenges pressing on families across the country. Turning Colorado around sends a message far beyond our borders: that courage still matters, that conviction still wins, and that ordinary Americans can reclaim their future. I believe deeply in that mission. I 've lived it my entire life. And I'm grateful to have so many determined patriots standing alongside me as we work toward a safer, stronger, more united future. | | For Colorado and for America, Victor Marx P.S. If you've gotten this far, there's something I need you to know: You 're not just someone reading an email -- you're someone whose voice and values matter deeply in shaping the direction of this mission. , and your involvement strengthens the momentum we've already begun to build. -Victor | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- | | PAID FOR BY VICTOR MARX FOR GOVERNOR Registered Agent: Gregory Carlson | | P.O. Box 156 Florence, CO 81226

name: Trisha Calvarese
subject: Trump’s $12b bailout for farmers is an outrage
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
Farmers want market access, not a handout to Big Ag: the rage, sadness, and betrayal is awful, and it is motivating a shift
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001AUnit_FezwRaiPli5zasnSchFh-U8C6-pTKBGuftxahCp1dGp9BO6mL12XPKRdBk1-9SaMLTWVGEsWiICOn3Qp_qL6uMSNX5YCQcE6won5irPAsDK6knF6w9HYEuC8z5l9o4dgn4yi1jCFM0VvArKnupc7UgEw45rlxZZeovmMzTB35L6rlRgbDwzpAE7Sv2x-LIrOeS0BGzlHFqqMnjzMqGlCAi0Iw7sno9iq6m9cY=&c=vzWfRImlQ7q5KoBmZaohKxV04yMgqmKluOT0ctbhInnUysS-KiCWAg==&ch=5f9XDxLblUv5k17n4HqPaFcVikcsjXd3_N1IPUZCZTJb-VN9bbMDGQ==)
---
|

Peter,
Many consultants have given me the same bad, unsolicited advice: focus only on wedge issues to fire up my base, and abandon any attempt at finding common ground with rural voters. I reject this false choice. And I hope you do too.
I have become radicalized for our rural communities. I care deeply about what happens to farmers and ranchers in this country--the people who work the land make up the backbone of my district, we all rely on their work to survive. Food isn't optional!
Donald Trump is destroying American agriculture. He is killing family farms and farming communities, decimating small towns, and ruining rural America with economic policies that only benefit billionaires and Big Ag.
Working people are not looking for handouts. They are looking for leaders who will tell the truth, recognize what is happening in this country that puts the American Dream out of reach for millions of us, and then DO SOMETHING about it.
So that's why I share my constituents' outrage and insult about Trump's pathetic "bailout" for Big Ag. Trump's policies are driving bankruptcy and despair. The depth of the rage, sadness, and betrayal so many are feeling in my district is awful, and it is motivating a shift.
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001AUnit_FezwRaiPli5zasnSchFh-U8C6-pTKBGuftxahCp1dGp9BO6mL12XPKRdBk1-9SaMLTWVGEsWiICOn3Qp_qL6uMSNX5YCQcE6won5irPAsDK6knF6w9HYEuC8z5l9o4dgn4yi1jCFM0VvArKnupc7UgEw45rlxZZeovmMzTB35L6rlRgbDwzpAE7Sv2x-LIrOeS0BGzlHFqqMnjzPovFY7lgLsp3B3NiG7aJJE=&c=vzWfRImlQ7q5KoBmZaohKxV04yMgqmKluOT0ctbhInnUysS-KiCWAg==&ch=5f9XDxLblUv5k17n4HqPaFcVikcsjXd3_N1IPUZCZTJb-VN9bbMDGQ==)
---

Trump only gets away with this because he owns and controls Republicans in Congress. And Trump's Enabler-In-Chief is my opponent, carpetbagger Lauren Boebert, who has wrecked my district in only 11 short months in office. Maybe if she were from here she'd care more about us.
I am building a massive grassroots machine that will get rid of Boebert for good. And I need you with me to get this done, for all of us.
Boebert is only in Congress because of a 546 vote margin. In 2024, as her Democratic opponent, I had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in my district, overperforming Kamala Harris by 6 pts as Boebert underperformed Trump in every single county.
[Will you please help me stand up for farming families, working people, and everyone who sees the damage Trump is doing to our country and wants to stop it? Right now, the best thing you can do is make a donation to keep our campaign on the ground and as strong as possible to defeat Lauren Boebert this election:](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001AUnit_FezwRaiPli5zasnSchFh-U8C6-pTKBGuftxahCp1dGp9BO6mL12XPKRdBk1-9SaMLTWVGEsWiICOn3Qp_qL6uMSNX5YCQcE6won5irPAsDK6knF6w9HYEuC8z5l9o4dgn4yi1jCFM0VvArKnupc7UgEw45rlxZZeovmMzTB35L6rlRgbDwzpAE7Sv2x-LIrOeS0BGzlHFqqMnjzCCv4kfM_rq5yOvGeNhujj4=&c=vzWfRImlQ7q5KoBmZaohKxV04yMgqmKluOT0ctbhInnUysS-KiCWAg==&ch=5f9XDxLblUv5k17n4HqPaFcVikcsjXd3_N1IPUZCZTJb-VN9bbMDGQ==)

body:
Farmers want market access, not a handout to Big Ag: the rage, sadness, and betrayal is awful, and it is motivating a shift
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wel49LUThloRb4leIWvDzpqwuVCvglD8WktLKzvVnACCOEv3vh9vlmOWgKqrpMhqnmv5kqlMAFhjMCtTWeM6Q6ZFD-IS7pM8NklzMU7mMJ2htwzQo7o8ieBU8a9MDJg8Y8lqa5Kf0aHdlBCWukE8mM2jLNRIt-OBzFwz4z2M_DbMZNrrE-8aIyhw_O4tDX2YpMA71SSeIFJqXlI1ksduRxn6VIN4q0_VJppdV8wEFyI=&c=_fOWNeTALRZdpjjEt0It9cTXfN1O-bDDy42mZrgPnrChNpHOU33SLQ==&ch=1bfrV9EkXwDwMraFRWCPTciw0R8JTTfYGSyRjyyL8vBZWsUt11BCLg==)
---
|

Peter,
Many consultants have given me the same bad, unsolicited advice: focus only on wedge issues to fire up my base, and abandon any attempt at finding common ground with rural voters. I reject this false choice. And I hope you do too.
I have become radicalized for our rural communities. I care deeply about what happens to farmers and ranchers in this country--the people who work the land make up the backbone of my district, we all rely on their work to survive. Food isn't optional!
Donald Trump is destroying American agriculture. He is killing family farms and farming communities, decimating small towns, and ruining rural America with economic policies that only benefit billionaires and Big Ag.
Working people are not looking for handouts. They are looking for leaders who will tell the truth, recognize what is happening in this country that puts the American Dream out of reach for millions of us, and then DO SOMETHING about it.
So that's why I share my constituents' outrage and insult about Trump's pathetic "bailout" for Big Ag. Trump's policies are driving bankruptcy and despair. The depth of the rage, sadness, and betrayal so many are feeling in my district is awful, and it is motivating a shift.
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wel49LUThloRb4leIWvDzpqwuVCvglD8WktLKzvVnACCOEv3vh9vlmOWgKqrpMhqnmv5kqlMAFhjMCtTWeM6Q6ZFD-IS7pM8NklzMU7mMJ2htwzQo7o8ieBU8a9MDJg8Y8lqa5Kf0aHdlBCWukE8mM2jLNRIt-OBzFwz4z2M_DbMZNrrE-8aIyhw_O4tDX2YpMA71SSeIFJqXlI1ksduRxFo2RMwRz1XNJqOTHQGkJQ=&c=_fOWNeTALRZdpjjEt0It9cTXfN1O-bDDy42mZrgPnrChNpHOU33SLQ==&ch=1bfrV9EkXwDwMraFRWCPTciw0R8JTTfYGSyRjyyL8vBZWsUt11BCLg==)
---

Trump only gets away with this because he owns and controls Republicans in Congress. And Trump's Enabler-In-Chief is my opponent, carpetbagger Lauren Boebert, who has wrecked my district in only 11 short months in office. Maybe if she were from here she'd care more about us.
I am building a massive grassroots machine that will get rid of Boebert for good. And I need you with me to get this done, for all of us.
Boebert is only in Congress because of a 546 vote margin. In 2024, as her Democratic opponent, I had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in my district, overperforming Kamala Harris by 6 pts as Boebert underperformed Trump in every single county.
[Will you please help me stand up for farming families, working people, and everyone who sees the damage Trump is doing to our country and wants to stop it? Right now, the best thing you can do is make a donation to keep our campaign on the ground and as strong as possible to defeat Lauren Boebert this election:](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wel49LUThloRb4leIWvDzpqwuVCvglD8WktLKzvVnACCOEv3vh9vlmOWgKqrpMhqnmv5kqlMAFhjMCtTWeM6Q6ZFD-IS7pM8NklzMU7mMJ2htwzQo7o8ieBU8a9MDJg8Y8lqa5Kf0aHdlBCWukE8mM2jLNRIt-OBzFwz4z2M_DbMZNrrE-8aIyhw_O4tDX2YpMA71SSeIFJqXlI1ksduR58aTwTt3PzsDS1bmk0EDAY=&c=_fOWNeTALRZdpjjEt0It9cTXfN1O-bDDy42mZrgPnrChNpHOU33SLQ==&ch=1bfrV9EkXwDwMraFRWCPTciw0R8JTTfYGSyRjyyL8vBZWsUt11BCLg==)

| Peter,
MAGA is getting smoked in states where it was once thought unimaginable. In yesterday's elections, Democrats broke the almost three decade Republican hold on Miami's mayorship and also took an unexpected ruby red State House seat in Georgia.
If deep red Republican strongholds like Miami and Georgia are in play for Democrats, that means swing states like Michigan and Arizona are on track to go deep blue. However, just like MAGA's plan to win the midterms by rigging them via redistricting, they've also got a plan to cheat their way out of losing states like Michigan, Georgia, and Arizona:
Stealing their state supreme courts via obscure judicial elections.
If these efforts are successful we could see something truly horrific happen on Election Night 2028.
Imagine, it's November 7th 2028 -- Michigan, Arizona, and Georgia, are all called for Democrats in landslide blue wave victories and a Democrat wins the Presidency.
However, in the following days state courts slowly but surely invalidate these Democratic victories citing a rehash of the 2020 election denial spiel (Soros, illegal immigrants voting, etc). Ultimately the Democratic presidential victory is nullified. Trump is declared the victor.
It sounds like a nightmare and too dark to be true but this is exactly why MAGA is working overtime to steal these courts. Defend our Courts just foiled one of these attempts in Pennsylvania. Now MAGA is attempting the same playbook in AZ, MI, and GA this 2026 and we must mobilize once again.
Obscure state supreme court races are not sexy, they are hard to fundraise for, but the stakes are life and death. name: Mark Ronchetti
subject: BREAKING: Keller wins — here's what it means for 2026
body:
Plus: Trump goes after insurance companies and Pantone's color is called racist | New for dpwillis67@gmail.com

| | | | | |
---
[|
---](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=55991e8e8a&e=567664f14c)
| | | | | |

Ok, Tuesday didn't go the way we hoped. Keller's still mayor. [But the real story isn't what happened — it's what's coming next.](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=8c1d1546e9&e=567664f14c)
We're breaking down why the environment is so brutal for Republicans right now, what it means for 2026, and why there's still reason for optimism down the road.
---
[|
---](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=d66fc2ba85&e=567664f14c)
| |
**🔥 WHY RESULTS DON'T MATTER ANYMORE**
This race wasn't about Darren White. It wasn't about messaging or fundraising or debate performance.
It was about one brutal reality: right now, party matters more than performance.
The Democratic registration advantage in Albuquerque is 20 points. The national environment is brutal for Republicans. And voters have locked into their teams like it's a sport — no matter what happens on the field.
We break down exactly why this race was always an uphill climb, what Darren said in his gracious concession, and APD Chief Medina's childish response that tells you everything about how the other side handles winning.

**⚠️ 2026 WARNING — BUCKLE UP**
New Jersey. Virginia. Tennessee. The pattern is the same everywhere.
When your party holds the White House, down-ballot races get harder. Much harder. Unless the economy visibly turns around, 2026 is going to be rough for Republicans across the country.
We lay out the timeline — and why Mark thinks 2028 flips and New Mexico elects a Republican governor by 2030.
---
| [Stream Episode Now](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=165c23919c&e=567664f14c)
---
| |
**⛽ GAS HITS $1.69 — LOWEST IN OVER FOUR YEARS**
Not all the news is bad.
Gas prices are plummeting. $1.69 in Denver. $2.95 national average. Even CNN admitted it's the lowest in 4.5 years.
Trump's energy strategy is working. We show you the clips — including California still stuck at $5 a gallon because of their regulations. That's where New Mexico is headed if the legislature gets its way.

**🏥 TRUMP GOES DIRECTLY AFTER INSURANCE COMPANIES**
"The Democrats are totally owned and bought by the insurance companies."
That's Trump making a populist healthcare play that's going to be very hard for the left to counter. We break down Rand Paul's Association Health Plan idea — letting individuals band together like Costco's 44 million members to negotiate rates.
And Krysty shares our own nightmare: our family's health insurance premiums now equal our mortgage. This issue is personal.
---
| [Stream Episode Now](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=e8a21d17e3&e=567664f14c)
---
| |
**🗳️ JASMINE CROCKETT'S SENATE ANNOUNCEMENT IS... SOMETHING**
The far-left Texas congresswoman just announced she's running for US Senate. Her launch video? Just clips of Trump calling her "low IQ" while she stares at the camera.
Colin Allred — the NFL player turned congressman who raised a ton of money against Ted Cruz — backed out of the race because of her.
Mark Halperin says she has no shot. We explain why she matters anyway — and what it tells you about where the Democratic Party actually is.

**🎨 PANTONE'S COLOR OF THE YEAR IS NOW "RACIST" APPARENTLY**
The 2026 color of the year is "Cloud Dancer." It's white. That's it. Just white.
Woke designers on TikTok are losing their minds. Calling it a "political statement." Comparing it to the Sydney Sweeney controversy.
We really do need to find these people something else to do.
---
|
---

✅ **RATE & REVIEW** us on [your favorite podcast platform](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=88ca68f4a4&e=567664f14c)
