body:
I deeply value your opinion, Fellow Conservative. No one understands what's at stake in the midterm elections better than you — because the outcomes will directly impact your daily life, from the cost of your groceries to the safety of your neighborhood. |
---
I deeply value your opinion, Fellow Conservative.

No one understands what's at stake in the midterm elections better than you — because the outcomes will directly impact your daily life, from the cost of your groceries to the safety of your neighborhood.

As the past Governor of South Dakota, I delivered real results by following conservative principles that don't fail.

Today, I'm bringing that same approach to Washington as your U.S. Senator, where I'm fighting every day to fix our broken government.

[**Before 2025 wraps up, I want to hear what's on your mind:**](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=952a558376474b25b27738a1b4da54df&_e=PGb93GW8w6UOAl964QqjAv0MsN0BYBOkepO9UeER4w4kouOmg-56hfmheG3e166456DdHRdIkVu0w5pEycw94yZWQNy76AoG7jIyK0zsnSYuXhgdukMa9kmniqWq8TwprpTkoTOH1wA1bJXP9Gc6N5-MIZXdtMlSBcS5L8cNSKOZSbCrSammF8QtiYvVo0irL3QxMonf3GJ26YKNKfirt5dFBZO2RpXnMZ_7Oy086A-QAjSl9z8nAyeBUwtJwjK546Y9Jq5r17xeRJr47MOT7pQvip__8_kKOFPZ7wCUo6sP-ZuhZ2OdRSKZJToME2DOclRUuV_g-qHNi40UuKDkWqWuQ3y1C1SJbiDTB_P_v61d-NYNCW5pBj9nZXUEdJXq-1HUR2R4I8p1kRTMF-ofUpyTZcIoKzD-vTyPKVHvIcxEYAzoezar9lxaJMmZrVEI4q8rHfOws2lPzvQcXAaaee5odHVaZaL8a4yJTySqF-JrjjUIhI592i_BY8xfLtpm8fbhehqFxg7rt1tTlirOGGH2_iSaFU5IpaNS4cO0ZNYkyNBTYvQkIZLzylBmHPA-j_lrPl4eT2UsDKTIobKrZ07Uy-Y67mha9MiCNstHUCrGz-GY_20EOSYm-2qc7djGqNb6BRfBBxfpCmulliEeuizEk4uOM5WTfVYOwTsLZ3yE5Xnrl8VGl4-3VGD9JVF2) How are things going for you? name: Maxine Dexter
subject: You made my birthday so special.

body:
https://maxinefororegon.com/ [https://maxinefororegon.com/]
Dear Peter,
Thank you so much for all of your beautiful birthday wishes.
When my team sent over the card, I didn’t realize they asked you to sign it as well. It was a truly wonderful surprise.

subject: MTG’s feud with Trump says everything about what’s

| This is what has defined our district for years:
---
| | [](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001_DRpPEKtn91hsVt1e4TrZomYQnvffd9yTuiiFgy14a7nuCCql9-SPzv6rBP5_NB5B7DgwzBqLn-XwFmB7_-Y6lT_hGeDiis78-bfOTdhKCC_L_T4ed28QPclDjUhH9kiH9PXZYssbaLswHWtxY2q53Ed-4CN5dLXdaGuFq7Bzih8jBbWIaVq_LQyYcYQstwX6w4uxUXo4jvN9rqun3rLkAwdMyRVxRAw&c=8wz1R-1qk1KoIivgmWQMQK0W7yH_umtI23buNSMl2XySXYZZxdWs0Q==&ch=SgpysmvHDFtDcRgYac3NrwiKGHoAaNQNj1JWSAspL_xrgD63XisKNA==)

| Drama, division, and constant national embarrassment -- instead of solutions, service, or stability.
But now, with Marjorie Taylor Greene resigning, we finally have a chance to change the story.
This district shouldn't have to worry about who their representative is fighting with, what feud they're starting, or which headline they're chasing next. Families here deserve a representative who shows up, listens, and puts people before politics.
And for the first time in a long time, we can build the GA-14 we want -- a district known for community, not chaos... dignity, not division... and leadership that brings people together instead of tearing them apart.
I'm running for Congress because I believe this moment is bigger than a resignation. It's a chance to reset. To rebuild. To prove that Northwest Georgia is ready for something better -- and that we can step into this new chapter with hope instead of fear.
[But opportunity only becomes progress if we organize now. Republicans will do everything they can to keep this seat locked in dysfunction. Grassroots power is how we break that cycle. Will you make a donation today to help us build a new future for GA-14?](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001_DRpPEKtn91hsVt1e4TrZomYQnvffd9yTuiiFgy14a7nuCCql9-SPzv6rBP5_NB5B7DgwzBqLn-XwFmB7_-Y6lT_hGeDiis78-bfOTdhKCC_L_T4ed28QPclDjUhH9kiH9PXZYssbaLswHWtxY2q53Ed-4CN5dLXdaGuFq7Bzih8jBbWIaVq_LQyYcYQstwX6w4uxUXo4js2AxDzMnheSAvKBQV3WQPU&c=8wz1R-1qk1KoIivgmWQMQK0W7yH_umtI23buNSMl2XySXYZZxdWs0Q==&ch=SgpysmvHDFtDcRgYac3NrwiKGHoAaNQNj1JWSAspL_xrgD63XisKNA==)

name: Stop ICE via Our Revolution
subject: Add your name: Stop ICE's reign of terror
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ stdin# ]Our Revolution Logo

Peter Willis,

In what can only be called an act of unchecked aggression, a federal ICE
agent just fired pepper spray at Congresswoman Adelita Grijalva during a
raid on a Tucson restaurant.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34854?source=email-20251210&t=1011&akid=9956%2E5885482%2EEv-U3N ]Headline: ICE goons pepper spray Congresswoman Adelita Grijalva during
Tucson raid

When Rep. Grijalva calmly identified herself and asked for clarification,
ICE agents pushed her aside and sprayed her in the face. “I was sprayed in
the face by a very aggressive agent, pushed around by others, when I
literally was not being aggressive, I was asking for clarification, which
is my right as a member of Congress,” Grijalva said.

Enough is enough. Congress cannot keep funneling billions in taxpayer
dollars to an agency that terrorizes immigrants, families, and even
members of Congress in plain daylight.

Rep. Grijalva is calling for transparency and accountability for ICE NOW –
let’s join her and overwhelm Congress with our demand that they stop
writing blank checks to ICE to terrorize our communities.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34854?source=email-20251210&t=1012&akid=9956%2E5885482%2EEv-U3N ]Enough is enough. Will you demand Congress reign ICE in NOW? Click here
to add your name.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34854?source=email-20251210&t=1013&akid=9956%2E5885482%2EEv-U3N ]ADD YOUR NAME

Let’s be clear: this is not an isolated incident. ICE’s violence is
standard operating procedure. But ICE agents are now brazen enough to
assault a sitting member of Congress, sprayed in public, simply for asking
questions on behalf of her community.

Imagine how ICE treats defenseless immigrants, refugees, and workers –
those without the privilege or power to speak out. If federal agents will
fire pellets at an elected official, there is no limit to the brutality
they inflict on everyday people. This cannot go on.

We are witnessing a crisis of accountability. ICE has become a rogue
agency, weaponized against immigrants, and emboldened to attack anyone in
their way – including our own representatives. Congress holds the power of
the purse. But instead of investing in community safety, education and
healthcare, our lawmakers are pumping BILLIONS into ICE’s budget to fund
raids, detention, and terror.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34854?source=email-20251210&t=1014&akid=9956%2E5885482%2EEv-U3N ]Rep. Grijalva on X denouncing ICE and their raids

Our Revolution stood with Grijalva when she ran for Congress. Then, as
House Speaker Johnson refused to swear her in for seven weeks, we came
together to demand she be sworn in. Now, we are standing with her again –
refusing to let Congress turn its back while ICE runs amok.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34854?source=email-20251210&t=1015&akid=9956%2E5885482%2EEv-U3N ]Stand with Our Revolution, Rep. Grijalva, and the millions who demand
dignity and justice. Tell Congress: Stop allowing ICE’s reign of terror.
Sign our petition now.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34854?source=email-20251210&t=1016&akid=9956%2E5885482%2EEv-U3N ]ADD YOUR NAME

When we organize, we win.

Our Revolution

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

name: Emily Randall
subject: In 2025, you kept me grounded
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress
body:
When I was elected, I promised to bring your voices – the voices of working people – into the halls of power in Congress. I’ve worked every day to live up to that promise.

| Peter, I'm watching through our [_**Year in Review video**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121009821/588059726/-980638963?v=UhMRpPvTGwQ&refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.12.10_EOYVidKick_90do_A&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NDZiZTc5Zi1lY2Q1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4DcW1KZ-Zt-ak3vUMmU8Vjr7ZR9-Cp-F2pYckufjUn0=&emci=deab3999-ecd5-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=946be79f-ecd5-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=23483442), and it’s hard to believe everything that happened in just twelve months! When I was elected, I promised to bring your voices – the voices of working people – into the halls of power in Congress. **I’ve worked every day to live up to that promise.** For the last year, I’ve had a front-row seat to our country’s monumental challenges: blind partisanship, blatant corruption, and the flood of Big Money into our political system. Throughout it all, your support, engagement, and feedback have kept me grounded and reminded me why I ran in the first place. clean_body: When I was elected, I promised to bring your voices – the voices of working people – into the halls of power in Congress. I’ve worked every day to live up to that promise. | Peter, I'm watching through our , and it’s hard to believe everything that happened in just twelve months! When I was elected, I promised to bring your voices – the voices of working people – into the halls of power in Congress. I’ve worked every day to live up to that promise. For the last year, I’ve had a front-row seat to our country’s monumental challenges: blind partisanship, blatant corruption, and the flood of Big Money into our political system. Throughout it all, your support, engagement, and feedback have kept me grounded and reminded me why I ran in the first place. Please watch and share our video, and if you’re able, = to fuel our organizing into 2026. We’re just getting started. Thank you so much 💙 – Emily = PREVIOUS EMAIL: Peter, we can’t believe we’re almost at the end of Emily’s first year in Congress! We put together a highlight reel from the past twelve months – please take a minute to watch and share: Some of our favorite moments include: ➡️ Connecting with thousands of neighbors at 16 town halls, both in-person and online. ➡️ Traveling to meetings across the 6th District by bus, bike, ferry, and even hiking trails. ➡️ Introducing House colleagues to Bad Bunny! 🐶 (He’s a big morale booster.) Make no mistake: This was a tough year for our neighbors and our country. Our team will be working hard over the next 11 months to hold the Trump regime and House Republicans accountable while also electing a new generation of leaders who will deliver for working people. But today, we hope you enjoy this compilation from this rollercoaster of a year – please like, comment, and share on your platform of choice! Thank you for being a part of Team Emily 💙 – the whole team at Emily Randall for Congress = | | Emily Randall is proud to represent Washington’s 6th District in Congress. She grew up in a working-class Union household in Port Orchard and was the first in her family to attend college. After serving in the State Senate and passing nearly 200 bills, Emily made history in 2024 as the first queer Latina elected to Congress. Now, Emily is fighting for WA working families in D.C. and organizing to take back the House. Let’s get real.

**Ilhan Omar, possibly the WORST person in Congress, wasn’t elected by Americans.**

But there is good news, Patriot.

We will defeat them in the ballot box.

Turning Point PAC’s ballot chasers are WINNING elections for America First patriots.

Non-Contribution Account (For TTPAC, Inc.’s Expenditures Other Than Direct Contributions to Federal Committees)

As permitted by law, TTPAC, Inc. maintains a non-contribution account for the purpose of influencing federal, state, and local elections. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, address, occupation, and employer of individuals whose contribution exceeds $200 in an election cycle. For contributions from federal committees, corporations, and labor unions - regardless of contribution amount - federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the committee’s name and address. State and local law may require us to collect and report additional information.

Contributions to this account may be used to influence federal elections by making independent expenditures in support of or in opposition to federal candidates. Additionally, in state and local jurisdictions that permit non-individual type (e.g., corporate) contributions directly to candidates and/or committees, contributions to this account may be used for both independent expenditures and direct contributions to candidates and/or committees, as permitted by state and local law.

Individuals, federal committees, corporations, and labor unions may contribute to TTPAC, Inc.’s non-contribution account without limit. Contributions by national banks, foreign nationals (without lawful permanent resident status), and other foreign entities (e.g., foreign corporations) are prohibited. Contributions to TTPAC, Inc.’s non-contribution account are not tax-deductible for federal income tax purposes.

body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121010043/588060416/536503440?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS85MDcyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YTMwMTc4MS1lZGQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=x5geGUlUnWFMfG5TfmeTkLFZVM0fdUCnubg_icfWpnQ=&emci=99033307-ecd5-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=8a301781-edd5-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=7878003)

My best friend died four years ago. My head was spinning. My heart was halved. I didn’t know what to do. But I did know that his daughter loved architecture. Distraught, I called up my friend Frank Gehry and asked for his advice. He said, “Why don’t you just send me her portfolio?” Then Frank talked to her and asked, “Why don’t you just work for me?” He mentored her every summer at Gehry Partners since. Frank Gehry, the most brilliant architect of our time, had the greatest eye and most visionary mind. But his heart? It towered over every magnificent building that will forever carry his name. When you talk about the most creative, world changing Californians of all time, I know Walt Disney, Steven Spielberg, and Steve Jobs may first come to mind. [_**But always, always remember Frank Gehry. I know I will.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121010044/588060417/1772266045?hl=en&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS85MDcyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YTMwMTc4MS1lZGQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=x5geGUlUnWFMfG5TfmeTkLFZVM0fdUCnubg_icfWpnQ=&emci=99033307-ecd5-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=8a301781-edd5-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=7878003) \- Eric

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

A fully loaded truck, flying down the interstate... no hands on the wheel, no one “in control” the way we’re used to.

No panic. No hesitation. Just a machine doing what humans used to do.

And [what happened next](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=ee66928c4a0a400db0f79406026947bd&_e=myDYc6s8Rdn5mvEM--1_G0-0dGQ-7Yafq8Z8IoGjobzHZRyQst7dADTXWMhRkbBGQWla9RWG3wJCpDhS4YxM2cyPqi-46hHIa7m0cOMQ9X7zCwqmQV0JmygCr-fki1EWJAEwF_el99tUj-C-VFAX9gAjHCSuMeWrzeBnyLY8Et24kkckMktxnNzOpEu2EdCtohrlu7dZB80bGkH-dgK9vhUZ6IRQ7RwPRE8V-wOzNRTurVgCO7NuIM6egysNkNSRd7x5_0ySI_ITrRoBpMb_QOnOQcPi4xTNFs_iKt2vcoYc7Yzm0erLEebvRDkTGN6cNW9pO5Te8Z3FQXOR-oKqriUc19JKlDkeuJthGxwBHKHlhHro97hyXyZ4t2cir6akg1nHj3lfzTIz88zQd8wKMZdR6_RoB_rnqe-3R1NTORJg_Ysy1zBXqsBn4R7ajhd6rVyctqmmP1eM8MIVxu3TACxHtX9RaqY1ANkyJc4DH-oIEofbsNRjxS-0uGNeWxLK-HPpvp6bjzTyINRNm1Vhg5lrs5AicgqWBJ0771sZtakmgEasX4qvOVGoNP4g7wxvT3JHWnZoYp7OzgoXfMKZnQ%3D%3D) changed my view of what “driving” means, what transport looks like, and how far tech has already gone.

You need to see this Nvidia’s trillion-dollar robot **before this kind of footage goes mainstream**.

[Watch this and tell me you’re not rattled.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=ee66928c4a0a400db0f79406026947bd&_e=myDYc6s8Rdn5mvEM--1_G0-0dGQ-7Yafq8Z8IoGjobzHZRyQst7dADTXWMhRkbBGQWla9RWG3wJCpDhS4YxM2cyPqi-46hHIa7m0cOMQ9X7zCwqmQV0JmygCr-fki1EWJAEwF_el99tUj-C-VFAX9gAjHCSuMeWrzeBnyLY8Et24kkckMktxnNzOpEu2EdCtXEnFJwSLFzrQNVwhRikAzoA7A_qP0IvUmLflm_NuCBV1vrvX-J5sKZUiEeqKdUudG4gfjwtn2cCCBoYea_FmLuUKipOPxCzkrtYDB6IX9StUnbgMEB5Qw1eQVJiUiEGlT-itFlTwdi6DB7OJ-z6-TWw4rmPowtzdqiUX8DwlAhaNiAYd20K02Cyod0x8r9jFw8DWXM4F7QEET8qdKDaUJZJ8GnpoXzbvAQPESKVT1yjpK4NEx7K6zawnpr4Zr7HqAam_E_UAkxgF2pyJ1w8pvhpwPU5n5-DgApXjI1dRpDGe2GB4aAiDfUd7CNjP3mvijNglqvTN_46pYT-0-L6wEMyMwT8q5LsZHi0npMDU4VkbUD1vVm8K_fUIrbmCRN11_ANeRXfqqBDcwbiP7S373g%3D%3D)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: No panic. No hesitation. Just a machine doing what humans used to do.| | | | December 10 | | | | | Self-Driving Truck Video Stunned Everyone | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. A fully loaded truck, flying down the interstate... no hands on the wheel, no one “in control” the way we’re used to. No panic. No hesitation. Just a machine doing what humans used to do. And changed my view of what “driving” means, what transport looks like, and how far tech has already gone. You need to see this Nvidia’s trillion-dollar robot before this kind of footage goes mainstream. Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: Cori Bush
subject: AIPAC picked a fight, now they’re gonna get one
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cori For Us
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251210_AIPACmessage_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251210_AIPACmessage_act]
AIPAC poured $9 million into St. Louis last cycle to lie about me and silence our movement.
But they made a mistake. They thought giving me time off would slow me down. They were wrong.
Now they’ve got a fight on their hands. I’m not just running for me, I’m running for all of us. For progressives, for the people, for everyone who stands up against the forces trying to crush progress. I’m running for YOU.
Last time I was in Congress, I flipped a few tables and ruffled some feathers in the name of good trouble on behalf of the people of St. Louis.
Now, we’re getting back up, stronger, louder, and ready to finish the work we started: lowering costs, protecting our neighbors, and standing up for St. Louis.
Our primary election is August 4, 2026 . It sounds far away, but I promise it’s closer than you think. Especially when billionaires are pouring money into my opponent’s campaign, hoping to drown out my voice again.
The reality is, it’s big money versus a movement they could never buy. We’re ready to win, and we need you on the ground, knocking doors, making calls, and connecting with voters.
Can I count on you to join the movement today by donating $25, or whatever you can afford, to be part of the fight to bring hope and dignity to families all across St. Louis? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251210_AIPACmessage_act&amount=25]

In solidarity,
— Cori
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251210_AIPACmessage_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251210_AIPACmessage_act] Cori Bush is a pastor, nurse, survivor, and formerly unhoused single mother of two who knows what it means to struggle and what it means to rise .
As a Congresswoman, she has always shown up when it’s hardest: securing disaster relief, lowering insulin costs, and delivering millions for our communities.
Across St. Louis, families are being priced out and pushed out. Cori Bush is fighting back for affordable housing, fair wages, and utilities as a human right. Be part of this people-first movement. Visit CoriBush.org [https://www.coribush.org] to learn more.

name: Chris Pappas
subject: I'm sorry about Karoline Leavitt.
body:
[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1001&akid=3944%2E582294%2ESbWr4k ]Chris Pappas for Senate[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1002&akid=3944%2E582294%2ESbWr4k ]Chris Pappas for Senate

I’m sorry about Karoline
Leavitt.

Before she was hand-picked as Donald Trump’s White House Press Secretary,
Karoline ran against me for Congress here in New Hampshire. She lost — by
nearly 10 points.

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251210_1_leavitt.hBPq_V&refcode=em20251210-1&t=1003&akid=3944%2E582294%2ESbWr4k ][IMG]

But that campaign gave her exactly what she needed: a test run for the
MAGA talking points she now repeats from behind the White House podium.
She’s been practicing the spin for years — and while New Hampshire wasn’t
buying it, Trump was.

Let me be clear: I don’t regret a thing about standing up to Karoline and
her MAGA agenda — not then and not now.

This race is going to be tough. National Republicans and John Sununu are
doing everything they can to flip this seat. They’re recycling the same
extremist ideas, running the same playbook and relying on people like
Karoline Leavitt to carry their message.

We beat her once and with your help, we’ll do it again.

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251210_1_leavitt.hBPq_V&refcode=em20251210-1&t=1004&akid=3944%2E582294%2ESbWr4k ]Will
you chip in $5 today to help us keep this Senate seat blue
and fight for what matters to Granite Staters?

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251210_1_leavitt.hBPq_V&amount=5&refcode=em20251210-1&t=1023&akid=3944%2E582294%2ESbWr4k ]Donate $5 »

Thanks for being in this fight with me.

Chris Pappas

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.chrispappas.org/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=3944%2E582294%2ESbWr4k

name: Senator Tim Sheehy
subject: We’re a nation divided
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TIM SHEEHY FOR MONTANA
body:
Can you answer some questions that will give our team a better insight into the nation's current mood? | [](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=3bd10a3313ae4cf7ae7f658728712110&_e=Ah2_7FFlTLzEeQaQk04fNikfu_EECVzDpxgBq4iZ94K06eib28YcvriQPq9mmsPf8CEA99DW0OkfSsEyYELvl1t2JP5jl3NBKeICHtxMDMJvbf0yUsiTsg1jPzF88BbLcmVq5yeJTtdz1oXLAJBCc8l7Ynfuns-fq071z7mLd2IEorn3qOBy_KOWt8pzKf0jAcpA2vWYryn-mOeYcKErjXQXnXJFZBQjyZYjoN3dC2O5qDjgHHMzgLil1l9kgI8hhtMK0O1C-t5mtIJJ6NFRewN_E7z2HPxaHyWQBux6Plt9_2SJnIyU2wVpOsfL661eDHKVM2fq6uS6tHfwj_jcVpSqxoBcVzybchA13gfBGDCUgHLAC4bdzNkDXrfypg91IMgxMxs_J6iKtrY12JClgz4mpG5fDmQYTUMQ3Z6KDF4izs8svYQ4zWTnSxHd8zQJWNxCLhExgMr0Y5-VYOdIHggF3qauxjvCq0qxfvDVhs2EdT7pID5Wip3Ii9LmF6p25NqRUyyS0mfN36KKElEcrHq13LLfJFbyfBPsozlz9Ak8hrNEWiDNagOX3W-TmDGiF1JjR6_RLSWX7TsuHncz-lC0bAiC_ts3lohZ7TXk61gD8w7eTYM2EeOOAW6lfYtt4Dvde5N9UOkQx0Zi7M-fDlr-YemUTN4zDcrusrRXZHzBesgaZF3N2HSaSIwJqCN01aFTYXel_z4LBL3VWb9yQoD-pDkfSr2I-UuIpErkKVo52A2EmuVbUP30MAx0keKX)

---
Fellow Conservative,

There is no doubt that we’re a country divided.

Democrats blame Republicans for what they say is the current dismal state of affairs in the country.

On the other side of the coin, Republicans blame Democrats for stalling progress, losing sight of what it means to be the “loyal opposition,” and throwing every roadblock in the way of President Trump and his administration that they can.

You know that I’m a Republican member of the Senate, and can infer what I think - or you can just visit my Twitter profile, my website, or my official Facebook page.

**But I want to know what YOU, Fellow Conservative,** think about the first 11 months of the Trump Presidency, the recent government shutdown, and, in general, the direction of our country.

[**cast your vote now →**](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=3bd10a3313ae4cf7ae7f658728712110&_e=Ah2_7FFlTLzEeQaQk04fNikfu_EECVzDpxgBq4iZ94K06eib28YcvriQPq9mmsPf8CEA99DW0OkfSsEyYELvl1t2JP5jl3NBKeICHtxMDMJvbf0yUsiTsg1jPzF88BbLcmVq5yeJTtdz1oXLAJBCc8l7Ynfuns-fq071z7mLd2IEorn3qOBy_KOWt8pzKf0jAcpA2vWYryn-mOeYcKErjXQXnXJFZBQjyZYjoN3dC2O5qDjgHHMzgLil1l9kgI8hhtMK0O1C-t5mtIJJ6NFRe9H5Q7HSa29FhQSKJRdfRm7JSB4YApTOn0SYD0hgIzUnqhSXjMEbsLgxhzaUcme-5v1cJBV3WnK3H-GDEB04qEyjznyeHh0WwQYFaOl9EPQ8sorRvONsYxchilT8E-iNgaQwgZfUwIlrZQYQiBHMcKWjrWsNXECyc0KpXfpSnA7GsvWD89it02u0_9SRnZl3CteBNOBAAuDG3zlk6xteRS3uZZsMVBuHqVSPFt0IvJqN4WMBcdWKC74S06MV0u2kb3r7BCxG33_z1D6aFnHq4eLV73Si5hScmkE8QnQ3_j-9ttlnLZtkRmFgylyQ7KR5DSA096rJdZQoKeA7_PBwCUBAwowAAByzJyFBsfxzlsCuRwC99pjfGYiDHN5UWIT-_doTnUGDntLYAaBDyG3RdxBB-GFFy6B8LoeY_qVdJ2EOb6-94k9reiVWju1vaZ7sBM8kECPAIOOOinTm2w2LuMCTEiSy7IEMF0dpX-rujtL0)

I’m hearing that a large number of voters are placing blame on Republicans for the recent 43-day government shutdown, but in all honesty, I’m struggling to see how.

clean_body: Can you answer some questions that will give our team a better insight into the nation's current mood? | Fellow Conservative, There is no doubt that we’re a country divided. Democrats blame Republicans for what they say is the current dismal state of affairs in the country. On the other side of the coin, Republicans blame Democrats for stalling progress, losing sight of what it means to be the “loyal opposition,” and throwing every roadblock in the way of President Trump and his administration that they can. You know that I’m a Republican member of the Senate, and can infer what I think - or you can just visit my Twitter profile, my website, or my official Facebook page. But I want to know what YOU, Fellow Conservative, think about the first 11 months of the Trump Presidency, the recent government shutdown, and, in general, the direction of our country. I’m hearing that a large number of voters are placing blame on Republicans for the recent 43-day government shutdown, but in all honesty, I’m struggling to see how. Are Democrats better messengers than we are? Do we need to refine our communication with the American people during times of national emergency? Last year, President Trump led Republicans to one of the most historic victories of our time. I know you don’t want to see our majorities in Congress crumble, so Thank you, Tim Sheehy U.S. Senator - Montana | " "I SERVED AS A NAVY SEAL, BUILT ONE OF THE LARGEST AERIAL FIREFIGHTING COMPANIES IN AMERICA CREATING OVER 200 MONTANA JOBS, AND STARTED A VETERAN-LED CATTLE RANCHING AND FEEDING OPERATION. | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

clean_body: Starting as soon as a few months from now, the United States government will make a sweeping change to bank accounts nationwide. | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | Starting as soon as a few months from now, the United States government will make a sweeping change to bank accounts nationwide. | It will give them unprecedented powers to control your bank account. Friend, did you know that 1 in 4 Tennesseans has medical debt? **That’s unacceptable. ** **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121011128/588063765/593011688?refcode=12.10.25_G4G_MED_DEBT_NATL&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODg3MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NTVhYjdkZi1mMWQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=MeNqk3WWb8mv9eHWeOqICTB3egq7pn-RWnS6J6Ztan8=)** That means that 1 in 4 Tennesseans has a medical debt that is negatively affecting their life and causing significant financial hardship — draining their savings, impacting credit, and creating housing instability. It also has profound effects on your mental and physical health, leading to increased stress, anxiety, depression, a higher risk of future medical issues, and a reluctance to seek necessary medical care. **We can do something about it. ** In Michigan, Governor Gretchen Whitmer championed a program that has erased over $144 million in medical debt for nearly 210,000 Michiganders. Governor JB Pritzker recently announced Illinois has erased $430 million in medical debt, benefitting 357,800 people. And in Arizona, Governor Katie Hobbs wiped out over $400 million in medical debt, bringing relief to 352,000 Arizonans. With hundreds of millions in our Tennessee Assistance for Needy Families reserves, we can erase millions of dollars in debt that are keeping Tennesseans financially strained and from seeking the care they need. **As Tennessee's next Governor, that’s exactly what I plan to do.** [**We need leaders who have the vision to build a better Tennessee--and country-- that works for everyone. And that’s exactly the kind of leader I will be for the state of Tennessee. clean_body: | Friend, did you know that 1 in 4 Tennesseans has medical debt? That’s unacceptable. = That means that 1 in 4 Tennesseans has a medical debt that is negatively affecting their life and causing significant financial hardship — draining their savings, impacting credit, and creating housing instability. It also has profound effects on your mental and physical health, leading to increased stress, anxiety, depression, a higher risk of future medical issues, and a reluctance to seek necessary medical care. We can do something about it. In Michigan, Governor Gretchen Whitmer championed a program that has erased over $144 million in medical debt for nearly 210,000 Michiganders. Governor JB Pritzker recently announced Illinois has erased $430 million in medical debt, benefitting 357,800 people. And in Arizona, Governor Katie Hobbs wiped out over $400 million in medical debt, bringing relief to 352,000 Arizonans. With hundreds of millions in our Tennessee Assistance for Needy Families reserves, we can erase millions of dollars in debt that are keeping Tennesseans financially strained and from seeking the care they need. As Tennessee's next Governor, that’s exactly what I plan to do. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Thank you for standing with me, for joining me in the fight for the people of Tennessee. Jerri Green For Governor P.O. Box 40034 Memphis, TN 38174 United States mailto:team@greenforgovernor.com | Paid for by Green for Governor. Hendrell Remus, Treasurer.

name: Lauren Toncich, Turning Point USA
subject: I've never written one of these to you
body:
The one event Charlie loved most...| | | [](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=d3e80b39244b48e4a4fc8d53c746823d&_e=v4jtsif2L2z8TEONXbs2VPyZ4vRK7peKuPugYAyBXaAF5v7WP3SkI94oR7L5eFABhLDuVe0rFvlkjOvnPOp4ND9PnXMANWV-7FwyedWqG2v1efVQjQJszlAQBRUdiyPTgZV6CEXUdkLFdUoq5JMCGJ-a1lrZ6YKbkBg4RNHRvpZGuBokwdnqEYnBRqjZM9dP8HG3Zm_uXZWcm_Fi4FOX4xbYmeWCggGSAPFWys22FL0YS4RPKG96XI4hG1QzZ0Ti4bk0hnNLv-qplxBEV4fr1381SX6YnjB-PCB70GHojAcY7gC937Nv-bP5Fq-Y91hY9HhbJsKH28FxCmh95b42tpp993HZkgBrfWds8ce-ajyFBR0OLafln2_wOxmgKDkUtMg3QdTLTc0_mHyohFCreGNdlB2YRYAvjwqMTd_6ECBWJsWLMnwkHJR_b01IYmFdVkiSpa89Oy2XGZ-eUrUtmTbWEKX9n0IR0mtwvsGrx8V29N_kuJgOobL5ZAb0xW_e16Y0d_cRlT_ivR_NyVwGh07zr0EWz7idBZSyfvSjVrFMumd1Dnp-01mcmxToWnYkbs9Z6ElNvZQo8G-aWGw4u8P--hWPg77fVFKL97UkQCEtdi2AYjUVpzzfd4p_C0WASQkfhbs8DD5RYpuDQG4G62oBc44FN1tdwwFaSaNVhid3XJN047icnbFkUqVctHP3)

| | Hi Fellow Patriot,

My name is Lauren Toncich -- and for the past seven years, I've helped lead the team behind the curtain at every Turning Point USA event.

Making sure the lights hit at the right moment, the music is pumping through the crowd, and tens of thousands of people walk into something unforgettable.

I've never emailed you directly before.

But if you've ever seen the opening night of AmericaFest... felt the electricity of a Student Action Summit crowd... or watched one of our massive rallies ignite a room...

Then you've witnessed the kind of energy Charlie dreamed of -- brought to life by our incredible events team.
---

At the heart of every single Turning Point event is this one truth: _Young Americans deserve a stage worthy of the fight they 're in. _

**And AmericaFest -- especially this year -- is our most important stage of all. **

**This December 18 -21 in Phoenix, thousands of students will walk into the most powerful conservative gathering in America. **

It will be our first AmFest without Charlie.

This is the stage he built -- with vision, grit, and sky-high standards.

Charlie poured everything into these events, because he believed students deserved more than beige ballrooms and lazy lectures.

He believed they needed to _FEEL_ the mission -- That they were part of something unstoppable... something big enough to change the world.

Backstage, I can still hear him directing the show: _" Louder music... more sparks... bigger energy... how full are we looking?" _

Not because he wanted flash for the sake of flash...

But because he knew energy moves people. Environments shape conviction.

_And atmosphere matters when you 're calling students into the fight of their lives. _

This year's AmFest?

_I 've never seen more students eager to be part of it. _

**Nearly 6,000 are registered. Another 2,200 are on the waitlist. **

**And we only have a few days left to get them in. **

Fellow Patriot, these students aren't looking for entertainment...

They're looking for courage, purpose, and a movement to belong to.

They're stepping onto hostile campuses. They're getting flak from Leftist professors.

Many are leading Turning Point chapters in places where they're told to sit down, shut up -- or worse.

**They want to stand for America... _They just need a chance to stand with others who will, too. _**

And when they get to AmFest, it's not just the main stage that changes their lives.

Behind the lights and cameras, they sit in smaller breakout rooms -- face-to-face with staff and speakers who show them exactly how to lead.

They're trained to fight -- and win.

Because inspiration is just the beginning. We want every student walking out of AmFest equipped for the battlefield they're stepping into.

clean_body: The one event Charlie loved most...| | | | | Hi Fellow Patriot, My name is Lauren Toncich -- and for the past seven years, I've helped lead the team behind the curtain at every Turning Point USA event. Making sure the lights hit at the right moment, the music is pumping through the crowd, and tens of thousands of people walk into something unforgettable. I've never emailed you directly before. But if you've ever seen the opening night of AmericaFest... felt the electricity of a Student Action Summit crowd... or watched one of our massive rallies ignite a room... Then you've witnessed the kind of energy Charlie dreamed of -- brought to life by our incredible events team. Above: That 's me with Charlie, backstage at last year's AmFest making sure every detail was perfect before showtime. At the heart of every single Turning Point event is this one truth: Young Americans deserve a stage worthy of the fight they 're in. And AmericaFest -- especially this year -- is our most important stage of all. This December 18 -21 in Phoenix, thousands of students will walk into the most powerful conservative gathering in America. It will be our first AmFest without Charlie. This is the stage he built -- with vision, grit, and sky-high standards. Charlie poured everything into these events, because he believed students deserved more than beige ballrooms and lazy lectures. He believed they needed to FEEL the mission -- That they were part of something unstoppable... something big enough to change the world. Backstage, I can still hear him directing the show: " Louder music... more sparks... bigger energy... how full are we looking?" Not because he wanted flash for the sake of flash... But because he knew energy moves people. Environments shape conviction. And atmosphere matters when you 're calling students into the fight of their lives. This year's AmFest? I 've never seen more students eager to be part of it. Nearly 6,000 are registered. Another 2,200 are on the waitlist. And we only have a few days left to get them in. Fellow Patriot, these students aren't looking for entertainment... They're looking for courage, purpose, and a movement to belong to. They're stepping onto hostile campuses. They're getting flak from Leftist professors. Many are leading Turning Point chapters in places where they're told to sit down, shut up -- or worse. They want to stand for America... They just need a chance to stand with others who will, too. And when they get to AmFest, it's not just the main stage that changes their lives. Behind the lights and cameras, they sit in smaller breakout rooms -- face-to-face with staff and speakers who show them exactly how to lead. They learn how to organize. How to launch and grow chapters. How to push back against the Left -- strategically and fearlessly. They're trained to fight -- and win. Because inspiration is just the beginning. We want every student walking out of AmFest equipped for the battlefield they're stepping into. Fellow Patriot, that 's where you can make a difference. Every $50 you give today helps sponsor a student to attend AmericaFest. And today, your gift will be MATCHED -- dollar-for-dollar. That means: 🔥 $50 sends TWO students 🔥 $100 sends FOUR 🔥 $250 sends EIGHT, and more. Every year, I see it firsthand: Students walk in with doubts, with fears, with barely enough money to afford the flight. They walk out with confidence, connection, and a new sense of calling. I've seen them go home and start chapters. I've seen them run for office. Many tell me -- even years later, that this one event changed everything. So no -- I'm not just asking you to sponsor an "event." I 'm asking you to help change the trajectory of a young patriot. Fellow Patriot, I know what Charlie would want right now. He'd want every seat filled. He'd want AmFest to echo with boldness and fire. He'd want us to fight like he's still backstage -- still calling for more sparks. Most of all, he'd want every student who made the trip to believe they were part of something bigger than themselves. To walk in searching for purpose -- and leave filled with it. . Let's make sure every young patriot who wants to fight for this country gets their chance -- and gets to Phoenix this December. | | For Charlie, Lauren Toncich Vice President of Events Turning Point USA P.S. This is our first AmericaFest without Charlie. But it will be the most important one we've ever hosted. Help us fill the room he loved -- with students ready to carry his mission forward. 4940 East Beverly Road Phoenix, Arizona 85044

name: Ro Khanna
subject: Real fighters, real The path to win in 2026 starts here
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ro for Congress
body:
https://rokhanna.com [https://rokhanna.com]
Hi Peter,
Too many Americans are losing faith that the government can deliver for them – basic needs like affordable housing, healthcare, childcare, and living wages. We can’t run the same playbook and expect different results. It’s time for new leadership.
So today, we’re rolling out our first 2026 endorsement slate: candidates who meet people where they are, listen first, and fight for solutions that actually improve the lives of working- and middle-class families. These aren’t paper endorsements; they’re a commitment to organize, register, turn out, and win.
Meet the slate:
https://x.com/RoKhanna/status/1998500325653614839 [https://x.com/RoKhanna/status/1998500325653614839]
This is how we win back working-class and young voters—by showing up, listening, and laying out policies that will improve people’s lives when we win:
✅ Increase Social Security and restore Medicaid funding
✅ Make billionaires pay their fair share
✅ Stop price-gouging on food and prescriptions
✅ Create a $17 minimum wage
✅ Invest in affordable housing
✅ Root out political corruption
But endorsements only matter if we have the resources to organize. We’re hitting the road for joint town halls, on-site voter registration, and a fall push to put these issues front and center.
Will you chip in $10 or more today to power this slate and help us register new Democratic voters where it counts? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2026-house-fundraiser?refcode=20251210-endorsement-slate&amount=10]

DONATE ANOTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2026-house-fundraiser?refcode=20251210-endorsement-slate]
If you can’t give, you can still help: share this slate, sign up to phone bank, or host a house meeting. Every action moves us closer to winning back the House and a new generation of leaders who will deliver for the American people.
Thank you for your support, your activism, and all that you do.
Onward,
Ro Khanna

name: Team Schiff
subject: Don’t forget to sign Eve’s card
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
|
# [](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/61?t=1&akid=9283%2E5257348%2E0hFFzQ)
Derek,
We’re celebrating the first anniversary of Adam’s swearing in as a U.S. Senator today, and we wanted to make sure you saw the email from Eve asking people to sign the card and share a word of support for Adam.
**[Will you take a moment to congratulate Adam on one year in the Senate?](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1024?c0=EG_2512_congratsAdam1.YCRZ8c&refcode=AU_ABS_EG_GEN_PET_AC90_20251210_AM1_V1_S1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&t=4&akid=9283%2E5257348%2E0hFFzQ)**
[SIGN THE CARD](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1024?c0=EG_2512_congratsAdam1.YCRZ8c&refcode=AU_ABS_EG_GEN_PET_AC90_20251210_AM1_V1_S1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&t=5&akid=9283%2E5257348%2E0hFFzQ)
---
Thanks,
Schiff HQ

_\---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Eve Schiff
Date: Tuesday, December 10, 2025
Subject: Sign my card to Adam_
Derek, this is Eve, Adam’s wife.
Tomorrow is the first anniversary of Adam’s swearing in to the U.S. Senate, and boy, what a year it’s been.
One year ago, our family gathered in the Capitol building to watch him take an oath of office we’ve seen him take before — but this time, in a new chamber.
But regardless of his title, the commitment is the same: to make a difference for hard-working families and stand up for our democracy.
I was so proud of Adam on that day, a feeling that has only increased over the last 12 months as we’ve watched him fearlessly stand up to Trump in the face of a slew of very personal, very vindictive attacks.
If anything, these attacks have strengthened his commitment to fighting for our country and fighting for you.
For as long as I have known Adam — in nearly 31 years of marriage — he has been guided by the belief that the people of this great country deserve the chance to get a good job, provide for themselves and their families, and enjoy a nice quality of life. That’s what he’s working for every single day, and he’ll keep up that fight.
**[Will you sign my card to Adam to congratulate him on one year in the U.S. Senate? This team means so much to him.](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1024?c0=EG_2512_congratsAdam1.YCRZ8c&refcode=AU_ABS_EG_GEN_PET_AC90_20251210_AM1_V1_S1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&t=6&akid=9283%2E5257348%2E0hFFzQ)**
[SIGN THE CARD](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1024?c0=EG_2512_congratsAdam1.YCRZ8c&refcode=AU_ABS_EG_GEN_PET_AC90_20251210_AM1_V1_S1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&t=7&akid=9283%2E5257348%2E0hFFzQ)
---
Adam and I share the belief that our country is full of promise and that our democracy is precious. And fragile. Adam has been on the frontlines, protecting it. He always will be.
Thanks for being a part of this,
Eve Schiff

name: Sarah McBride
subject: Patients, caregivers, and providers need a seat at the table.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
We need to take on the system that too often puts corporate interests ahead of everyday people.
#
In the wealthiest nation on earth, any person left without health coverage is a moral failing.
For far too many people in this country, our health care system can feel designed to deny and delay care, raise the price on medication, or hit you with a surprise bill after you’ve done everything “right.”
**That’s not a health care system — it’s a sickness system.**
Here’s what I believe: we have to take on the system that too often puts corporate interests ahead of everyday people.
When insurance companies, drug manufacturers, and giant health care conglomerates have the most influence in the lawmaking process, the result will always be higher costs, fewer choices, and people forced into impossible decisions about their health.
But a system that _actually_ puts people first starts with universal coverage as the goal — and real, concrete steps to get there like protecting and expanding access, lowering costs, and strengthening Medicare, including dental, vision, and hearing, while ensuring no one is priced out of the care they need.
**And to build that kind of system, the people driving the conversation have to be the people living it — patients, caregivers, and providers — not the industries profiting off of people getting sick.**
But taking on the insurance industry and Big Pharma won’t be easy. It takes a lot of resources to fight back against billionaires who are ready to pour in whatever amount it takes to protect their profits.
That’s why I need your support today.
**[Will you chip in $5 to help me fight for a health care system that works for everyone?](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-20251210-1.nwtLZo&refcode=em20251210-2572&amount=5&t=1&akid=2572%2E21716%2EP5uaAN)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-20251210-1.nwtLZo&amount=5&refcode=em20251210-2572&t=20&akid=2572%2E21716%2EP5uaAN)**
Thank you for your support,
Sarah McBride
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-footer?refcode=em20251210-1&t=21&refcode2=2572_21716_P5uaAN&akid=2572%2E21716%2EP5uaAN)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/receive_fewer_emails/?t=22&akid=2572%2E21716%2EP5uaAN)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/update_your_information/?t=23&akid=2572%2E21716%2EP5uaAN)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=24&akid=2572%2E21716%2EP5uaAN)

Email us: [info@sarahmcbride.com](mailto:info@sarahmcbride.com)
Paid for by McBride for Delaware

name: Jimmy Panetta
subject: I can’t stand the high grocery prices from tariffs
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jimmy Panetta for Congress 2026, FEC ID# C00592154
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121014403/588075347/-2113420158?source=EM_NBI_JP_20251210_FR_Tariffs_0_1MO_JimmyPanetta_NA_NA_NA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTkwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NjY4YmE4Mi1mNmQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=m7lw3Mfkkk_0XUpJP0-u--jX-hz0l8A-PrUfW73z-A0=&emci=412b7a7c-f6d5-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=9668ba82-f6d5-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=6108787) | Peter,

Pasta and spaghetti are staples in my Italian-American household. But what we don’t like — what I can’t stand — are the high prices associated with not just pasta, but groceries across the board.

This is what happens when the Trump Administration fixates on tariffs: **hardworking Americans suffer and have to pay the price.** That means higher grocery costs, a higher cost of living, and families struggling to make ends meet.

**That’s why I’m fighting to bring back Congressional control over tariffs and trade.** The Constitution gave trade and tariff policy authority to Congress. **You** deserve representation and a voice in our trade and tariff policy — _especially when it’s raising grocery prices in an economy that Americans are already struggling in._

[Fighting back takes all of us, Peter. With a strong grassroots movement behind me, we can fight, together, from a stronger stance. Will you show just how strong and determined we are in fighting for an affordable future?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121014404/588075348/-840681789?amount=10&source=EM_NBI_JP_20251210_FR_Tariffs_0_1MO_JimmyPanetta_NA_NA_NA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTkwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NjY4YmE4Mi1mNmQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=m7lw3Mfkkk_0XUpJP0-u--jX-hz0l8A-PrUfW73z-A0=) | [CHIP IN TODAY!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121014405/588075349/-840681789?amount=10&source=EM_NBI_JP_20251210_FR_Tariffs_0_1MO_JimmyPanetta_NA_NA_NA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTkwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NjY4YmE4Mi1mNmQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=m7lw3Mfkkk_0XUpJP0-u--jX-hz0l8A-PrUfW73z-A0=)

I’m fighting in Washington to reduce _all_ costs for _all_ Californians. I’m not just pushing back against this reckless tariff policy — **I’m fighting for an affordable future for all of California’s working families.

clean_body: | Peter, Pasta and spaghetti are staples in my Italian-American household. But what we don’t like — what I can’t stand — are the high prices associated with not just pasta, but groceries across the board. This is what happens when the Trump Administration fixates on tariffs: hardworking Americans suffer and have to pay the price. That means higher grocery costs, a higher cost of living, and families struggling to make ends meet. That’s why I’m fighting to bring back Congressional control over tariffs and trade. The Constitution gave trade and tariff policy authority to Congress. You deserve representation and a voice in our trade and tariff policy — especially when it’s raising grocery prices in an economy that Americans are already struggling in. = | = I’m fighting in Washington to reduce all costs for all Californians. I’m not just pushing back against this reckless tariff policy — I’m fighting for an affordable future for all of California’s working families. = Jimmy If you'd like to receive only the most important updates from us, Authorized and Paid for by Jimmy Panetta for Congress 2026, FEC ID# C00592154 Jimmy Panetta for Congress PO Box 103 Carmel Valley, CA 93924 United States ** **

name: VoteVets.org
subject: Tell Hegseth: RELEASE THE DAMN VIDEO
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
Hegseth couldn’t wait to show off the video showing his first illegal boat strike in September. Posted it on social media, gave it to cable news, went and did interviews about it, started putting together tough guy videos and dumb memes.
#
Peter —
Hegseth couldn’t wait to show off the video of his first illegal boat strike back in September. Posted it on social media, gave it to cable news, went and did interviews about it, started putting together tough guy videos and dumb memes.
The guy wouldn’t shut up about it.
But there’s more video, and it shows further strikes on shipwrecked survivors. Lawmakers were horrified after they saw it. It has been described and profiled in the press. Trump himself said he’d be fine with it being released.
But now Hegseth doesn’t want anybody seeing the rest of the footage. He’s adamant these strikes were not war crimes — but he’s doing everything he can to cover up the evidence that would let people decide for themselves.
Nah. That’s not going to cut it.
If this is the supposed most transparent President, White House, and Defense Department in American history, they should **release the video.**
If these strikes were defensible in any way, **release the video.**
If they have nothing to hide, **release the damn video.**
This is STILL a democracy, and the American people deserve to know the truth about what Hegseth is ordering our military to do.
[**We are demanding that the Department of Defense release the footage of these strikes with all haste, and we’re asking you to sign on.**](https://act.votevets.org/signup/251210_vv_em_hegseth_video/?source=em251210-1-160&akid=34814%2E3013755%2Ee7bThl)
| [RELEASE THE DAMN VIDEO](https://act.votevets.org/signup/251210_vv_em_hegseth_video/?source=em251210-1-160&akid=34814%2E3013755%2Ee7bThl)
---
Let the people decide.
Please, sign on.
The team at VoteVets
